steps.name: Set Up
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qaKDaV2"}},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bYlcK49"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PM to complete Job Info tab in change order. * "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bYlcK49"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The \u201cRef#1\u201d column will be the RFI,ASI,PR,RFQ number received from the GC, the description will be the description of work. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bYlcK49"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Status will be \u201cPricing\u201d from the drop down when the CO is first entered, this will change to \u201cPotential\u201d, \u201cNTP\u201d, etc. as it moves through the status phases. \u00a0"}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bYlcK49"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Client Report Style will be \u201cClient Report 5.7.21\u201d. This can be modified to show as much or as little detail required for each project. Get with your PX if a different report style is needed. "},{"type":"text","text":"DO NOT ADJUST THE CLIENT REPORT.","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Doing so will change it for all other projects."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-3rzMScc"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-EjVyjlq"}}]}

steps.name: Notes Tab
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bAAQFHr"}},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-VgqtJcg"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PM to enter document number for reference (i.e. ASI, RFI, PR, etc.) Under the \u201cReference\u201d they should type the description of the change order with some detailed information. If there are long lead items, type them in this area with red text. The reservation of rights has been approved by the TE president and needs to stay included on every change order, including the \u201cgood for acceptance\u201d date. The time request should be adjusted to each CO to make sense but needs to be in the notes."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-yB5Z11x"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 CLARIFICTIONS:","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1IS7ReJ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 Reservation of Rights","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-nuUjdj7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 This COR does not include any amount for impacts such as interference, disruptions, rescheduling, changes in the","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Dq12sPa"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 sequence of work, delays and\/or associated acceleration. We expressly reserve the right to submit our request for","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BBqL6hI"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 any of these items.","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uyo1iEQ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 *Not responsible for damages to ceiling grid, ceiling tile, sheetrock, painting, or tile.","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-nLFOkkj"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 *This price is good for acceptance within 10 days from the date of receipt.","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LdVjDeF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0 *We request a time extension of 3 days.","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]}]}

steps.name: Take Off Tab
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-oiZJ6Yz"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2GrzcRH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The takeoff is already input by the estimator unless it\u2019s a small CO that the PM completed. If there are counts or lengths that need to be adjusted per field requirements, do it here. Go over this with your Foreman\/Superintendent for accuracy and field conditions. To add items, most of the time it will be from the \u201cItem Database\u201d in the \u201cTake Off\u201d drop down. Estimating has saved prebuilt assemblies for \u201cStub Ups\u201d in the \u201cHeating\u201d drop down and branch conduit runs in the \u201cTemporary Assemblies\u201d drop down. If you need to see what\u2019s in each assembly or adjust it, right click on that assembly and click \u201cmodify\u201d. You can add any of these assemblies buy double clicking and making any adjustment necessary to make this assembly match your project. This does not adjust the data base, just your project. You can also build your own temporary assemblies for your project if this saves you time or you have a specialty item that is not in the data base. Again, only you will see the assemblies you build and this will not affect the actual database. DO NOT ever go into Global defaults for any reason. Global defaults will only be modified with the VP of estimating\u2019s approval."}]}]}

steps.name: Extension Tab
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Pv4NPwu"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Mv2uhSk"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"1-\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 Make sure that the link price has been updated or the estimator has changed it in the \u201ctrade price\u201d column. If it has been adjusted, the color of the dollar value text under link price and trade price will be blue. If the estimator did not run the link price, you must run it or ask estimating to do this."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-rKqndxQ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"2-\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 On conduit and wire, check the price of our buy out against the current commodity pricing sheet to make sure we are using the higher of the two. On deducts we should be using the lowest of either the bid day cost, TE buy out or commodity pricing sheet."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-evtB9jf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"3-\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 Make sure that there is "},{"type":"text","text":"30% in the \u201cAdj %\u201d column","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":". This adds 30% to the net cost of material and does not show up on the client report."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-QKakyct"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"4-\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 If counts or lengths are incorrect adjust them on the \u201cTakeOff\u201d tab, not the extension tab."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BLTeK5u"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"5-\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0 On the extension tab, drop down extension and click on labor column. Make sure the appropriate labor column is being used for the project. NECA Difficult should be used on most projects, many will be at NECA Very Difficult, this should be set up in the \u201cProject Default\u201d and approved by the PX\/Pesident. Be sure to go through labor units to make sure there are none that are blank. If they are blank, insert NECA values used on the project in the \u201cLabor Column\u201d in the extension tab."}]}]}

steps.name: Direct Labor Tab
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2YMRD1u"}},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cSr4QBt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Make sure that your labor rate is correct per the approved parameters. This rate should be preset but check it to be sure. Make sure you are looking at the \u201cTotal\u201d column, you must have the rate typed in the total column as all other columns are just used for calculations. "}]}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cSr4QBt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you are charging 20% of the labor to overtime, change the 100 under \u201cCrew % Straight Time\u201d column to 80 and type in 20 in the overtime line. Straight \"Crew\" rates and overtime rates as well as working foreman, superintendent etc. should be set by President and PX."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cSr4QBt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Most projects can get 10% for a \u201cworking foreman or superintendent\u201d, this is to go under the \u201cDir Lab\u201d (direct labor) tab, not \u201cInd Lab\u201d (Indirect Labor) tab. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cSr4QBt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Most projects will use a \u201cCrew Rate\u201d as opposed to separate Journeyman and Apprentice rates. If separate rates are required then this is where you would enter those rates. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cSr4QBt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once this is decided you or your PX will go into the \u201cEdit Project Default\u201d and save all of this info along with burden and fringe if required to show it. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cSr4QBt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"DO NOT ever go into Global defaults for any reason. Global defaults will only be modified with the VP of estimating\u2019s approval."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-o5tSmt8"}}]}

This will be set by the President."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Sn2SJDa"}}]}

steps.name: General Expenses Tab
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Cp97n1t"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-viDbj48"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This tab allows you to charge for General Expenses if allowed per sec. Most spec takes exception to general expenses with the exception of 5% \u201cConsumables\u201d that some allow. This will be on the Change Order Set Up Template that should have been previously approved by the TE President. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-viDbj48"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Do not put equipment under general expenses, equipment goes under the equipment tab."}]}]}

steps.name: Equipment
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-9lm8Wgi"}},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-NsnaluK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This tab is for any additional equipment needed for the change order. Whether we have it on the project currently or not, there needs to be a charge if its needed. There should be taxes set up at 8.25% in the tax column but this is marked up on the final totals tab so no mark up or overhead needed here."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-oGh4kjg"}}]}

"},{"type":"text","text":"Make sure that the allowed markup per spec is in the MU% column. This number IS NOT marked up in the final pricing, it MUST go here.","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Be sure to choose the proper \u201cSub Contractor ID\u201d here so that it shows up on the schedule as a cost owed to that Sub Contractor."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7HO7BRs"}}]}

steps.name: Quoted Material
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-YEofRiz"}},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-c5BcKyY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is where all fixture, gear, cable tray, generator or any other \u201cHard PO\u201d quotes go. Be sure to choose the proper \u201cQuoted Material ID\u201d here so that it shows up on the schedule as a cost owed to that Vendor. Make sure the cost code in the code column is correct and what you plan on charging it to or cutting a PO revision for."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JjOtNDA"}}]}

steps.name: Totals
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-G1wzl66"}},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-RPuiuxj"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This is where the markup, warranty at 2% and P&P bond percent go if allowed and or required. This should have been pre approved by the President in a previous step. It allows automatic markup based on a set percentage, progressive or breakpoints depending on what the CD\u2019s allow. The final adjustment row should be used to \u201cround\u201d to the nearest dollar so that the CO price has no cents unless the GC requires to the penny totals."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-XPqJ6EN"}}]}

steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-F6o7PHo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Policy for Publishing Unapproved Construction Drawings","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ac3nSU6"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Purpose","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kWIaGWm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This policy outlines the procedures and guidelines for handling and publishing construction drawings that have not received final approval. It aims to ensure that all stakeholders are aware of the status of drawings and that unapproved drawings are managed with appropriate caution to prevent misuse, miscommunication, and legal issues\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-0DD6T2t"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Scope","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LGU4Hm9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This policy applies to all employees, sub-contractors, and other stakeholders involved in the preparation, review, and distribution of construction drawings. It covers all stages of the construction design process, from initial drafts to final approval.\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DkeV1KC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Policy Statement","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-0L9ooi8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Under no circumstances should unapproved construction drawings be published, distributed, or used for construction without adhering to the guidelines set forth in this policy. Unapproved drawings represent work in progress and are subject to pricing revisions and approval. Therefore, any unauthorized use or distribution of such documents can lead to significant risks, including but not limited to miscommunication, construction errors, legal liability, and financial losses.\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-9Sr9v1A"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Definitions","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]},{"type":"italic"}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qaswSRA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Unapproved steps.name: Create RFI
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-dXfJlb9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"For RFI Manager"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-IaE69pF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"You can create an RFI as a Draft or for Submission. If you create it in draft form you can edit the RFI from the RFI Log prior to sending. If you create it n Open - For Reviewers it will be immediately sent to the Reviewer (GC) and it's status will be updated as \"In Review\" in the log."}]},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20240524_181959_mi_0qns1k0tfhvi1h4jchxlw3f2o8an7y1zn_ib0r3xnway9v.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20240524_181959_mi_0qns1k0tfhvi1h4jchxlw3f2o8an7y1zn_ib0r3xnway9v.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-ihsvNcl"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-jJtUtjI"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"For All Others"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-23YV3HK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"You have a choice to create the RFI as a draft, which can be edited later or as Open - It aims to ensure that all stakeholders are aware of the contract requirements for commissioning."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kwM1ati"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kwM1ati"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Scope","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-FnDZudq"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This policy applies to all employees involved in recording and submitting the commissioning items to the client."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4qxctef"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4qxctef"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Policy Statement","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-snWn3LN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"While it is the entire team's responsibility to complete the commissioning documents, the commissioning log should be created early in the project so that all employees, office and field, are aware of the requirements of commissioning prior to installation. steps.name: View Logs
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Ahsj3qn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"To view logs"}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Ahsj3qn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Click on the project you would like to update, any logs associated with this job will drop down. Note-closed jobs CANNOT be edited and will no longer appear on this list."}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Ahsj3qn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"An icon will appear to the left of the project name and to the left of the specific QMS log appearing like a piece of paper, this is your edit icon."}]},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185339_mi_0qxkom3l2cuamwtsxdtd7yj8svadqxpjn_5b94s9xkkzn5.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185339_mi_0qxkom3l2cuamwtsxdtd7yj8svadqxpjn_5b94s9xkkzn5.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-ZOhDupc"}},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4Xd4InW"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"To look at the job as a whole, and edit within, click on the edit icon to the left of the "},{"type":"text","text":"project name","marks":[{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" (see above.) This will bring you to another page and will list all the QMS logs (see below.)"}]},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185400_mi_0qxkomkztebxil88ujiux24wr54aixuma_a1dx5yveot2l.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185400_mi_0qxkomkztebxil88ujiux24wr54aixuma_a1dx5yveot2l.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-fFdp86Y"}}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CmfXmCJ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"To directly edit a specific QMS log within this project, click on the edit icon to the left of the specific QMS log (see below.) steps.name: Add Logs
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-yArnaOx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"To add a QMS log, "}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-yArnaOx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Click Download Template"}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kuTA2PP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Update your template as needed, and return to the QMS software. Click Upload Template, and find the template you just edited, and upload it. I HIGHLY recommend once you have finished your edits to run the project report to see edits within. These will need to be corrected, so to get ahead of them if you run it while it is fresh it will help navigate (see step 5 REPORTS for instructions.)"}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-tQdaquN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Please note, only one person can edit a template at a time, however the program allows more than one person to download a template at the same time. This means, if person A downloads a template at 5:00PM and started to edit, and person B downloads a template before person A uploads theirs it has now been replaced by person B\u2019s download and is no longer valid."}]}]}]}]}]}]}]},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185622_mi_0qxkopxemigs0bcbqc088uevvu6a99qcq_u4x7hwar35jo.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185622_mi_0qxkopxemigs0bcbqc088uevvu6a99qcq_u4x7hwar35jo.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-WaG5Wxp"}},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185631_mi_0qxkoq46i7r0hasqyjxdqotif649k5y5q_qkefgeenjidr.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_185631_mi_0qxkoq46i7r0hasqyjxdqotif649k5y5q_qkefgeenjidr.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-JevJaBa"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kKb0x4N"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-VXFaZVY"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-a0mss3n"}}]}

steps.name: Reports
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Vw1brJ7"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-prKQ81k"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reports-","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Will have reports available to see any issues within a project."}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-rB8dDM8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Click on Reports which will drop down any reports available to run, in this case only Project Report is available."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Oiceg2T"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Select the Project Name, QMS log and the status you are wanting to generate a report from.\u00a0 A drop down of selections will appear. QMS log and Status drop downs do have the capability of selecting all or specific selections."}]}]}]},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191152_mi_0qxkpcl7vaopssrpkp95dlvdawqmhs6be_bfsyfn64cb5u.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191152_mi_0qxkpcl7vaopssrpkp95dlvdawqmhs6be_bfsyfn64cb5u.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-c4Cgxc5"}},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191158_mi_0qxkpcq0ganrbh0s6msfxjwfthpileqwu_n6znvdfyfv3u.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191158_mi_0qxkpcq0ganrbh0s6msfxjwfthpileqwu_n6znvdfyfv3u.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-2PyRgXQ"}},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191204_mi_0qxkpcvwknxqhdkujo79p7w1a9wh6b0rc_ptxedgjf3yrp.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191204_mi_0qxkpcvwknxqhdkujo79p7w1a9wh6b0rc_ptxedgjf3yrp.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-ZeDyZRg"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SQRigdT"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-q4hZtvK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you want your report to only reflect those with issues, toggle over on With Issues Only (see below.)"}]},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191247_mi_0qxkpdvz5ncv7kxk8ajrftucxhy2upts0_tjcvj28fdqoq.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191247_mi_0qxkpdvz5ncv7kxk8ajrftucxhy2upts0_tjcvj28fdqoq.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-VuwyuoT"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-V9daw0w"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5CYeTVO"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Click Generate Report once you are ready. This will list your desired responses below (see image above.)\u00a0You can also export to Excel by clicking the To Excel button (see image above in ii.)"}]},{"type":"image","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191349_mi_0qxkpfbowawbhgejgoxyw5qytdqwlu5cc_4xuyasyk3z6s.png","alt":"","asset":"https:\/\/waybook-spaces-production.fra1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com\/uploads\/t_0qd67svcvqonhywlbap1oo2tita9u7cd7t\/20241127_191349_mi_0qxkpfbowawbhgejgoxyw5qytdqwlu5cc_4xuyasyk3z6s.png","title":"","width":"100%","id":"pm-d4TOF3b"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bim5xuV"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-GfP5rhe"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once your report is generated it will appear below the selection options. The example below shows one where all QMS logs are selected, and all statuses. Each log you can select and the statuses will appear. Issues will be displayed to your far right."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-viwwegT"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-viwwegT"}}]}

steps.name: Issues
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-XqorzpS"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Issues:","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" there are different issues that may appear after you upload your template. Below is a guideline of how to navigate issues that are showing.\u00a0\u00a0"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DE5T5JP"}},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-m8Em76h"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Columns N & O not filled out. If you are adding a log that was completed with the old process for the first time, make sure columns N & O are filled out.\u00a0\u00a0"}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-xXVQiPh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"You will copy these from the previous reports "},{"type":"text","text":"despite","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":" these noting they are to be filled out by TE employee.\u00a0"}]}]}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iFGTbNB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hold Status-If the status is set to Hold and the "},{"type":"text","text":"Drop Dead","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" "},{"type":"text","text":"Date to Release Material","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" is less than 14 days away from today (or before), this will trigger an issue."}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5Vfewrb"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Drop Dead Date to Release Material is found on the report generated\/excel export of this report. This date is generated from Projected Lead Time entered, minus Date Material Due on Jobsite Per CPM Schedule."}]}]}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-PEvkLRJ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Released Status- If the material is in Released status and the "},{"type":"text","text":"Date Material Due on Jobsite Per CPM Schedule","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" is "},{"type":"text","text":"before","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","text":" the "},{"type":"text","text":"Projected Date to Receive Material","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" at Vendor WH, then this material will trigger an issue."}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SDmA89N"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Projected Date to Receive Material at Vendor WH is found on the report generated\/excel export of this report.\u00a0 The date is generated as one week after Initial Ship Date Provided from Factory to Vendor."}]}]}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-h65dnU8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If the "},{"type":"text","text":"Date Material Due on Jobsite Per CPM Schedule","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" and "},{"type":"text","text":"Date TE Authorized Material to be Released","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"underline"}]},{"type":"text","text":" are not filled in, then this will create an issue.\u00a0"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Qo9egWO"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Stored location has to have a location set, or this will create an issue."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-xXVQiPh"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cDivQ3M"}}]}

steps.name: 6/18/24 - Members, Notifications, & Files
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-Jhyqv4Y"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-d2mW0eT"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/kn8pWVqlK90\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"29bacd383e8199de5446ad3b6f291458f97ac31bdfa1c736652575ff754a8047","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-Dsq34p3"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-JKyHIUW"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Field Roles"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZIGrYqp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"See attached for field roles"}]},{"type":"attachment","attrs":{"src":"https:\/\/tumlinson-electric.waybook.com\/api\/media-items\/mi_0qp0knyv2rnauo8gm8wpps3i8ocg9vmrq\/_download","extension":"xlsx","filesize":19933,"mimetype":"","name":"Field User Roles.xlsx","id":"pm-daVtfWt"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iB5zGxD"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-Pbcga2Y"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hg6Hvki"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Presentation of Build Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-KPL2ovV"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a presentation on the Build process, stressing the importance of ensuring that project settings are correct. He highlights the three main topics to be covered: members, notification, and files, and urges the team to review their projects and make necessary adjustments based on the information provided in Waybook. Justin Tumlinson also clarifies the need to publish settings in Waybook for everyone to access."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WL8vpXf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Project Permissions and Roles"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JzbJY61"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides detailed information on project permissions and roles, highlighting the specific roles of project administrators, BIM cart, field foreman, and site superintendent. He stresses the significance of setting permissions based on roles to maintain consistency and control access to project files. Additionally, he mentions the need to ensure correct settings for file structure and members across all projects by the next training session."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-fT3lpoZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Members and their roles"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-45UUHw9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* File permissions and settings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4ZGIum1"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Role-Based Notifications and Group Settings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-U5pztY4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed overview of the role-based notifications and group settings, highlighting the creation of various groups such as BIM, Field, Vendor, PM, Estimating, and Client groups. He explains the notification settings for each group and the flexibility to adjust settings based on specific needs, while also mentioning the power of notifications for effective communication within the team."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8SwugUW"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Notifications and Templates in Waybook"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-0Cv1AhH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a discussion on minimizing nuisance notifications and providing manageable information to the team. Eric Middaugh inquires about the notification setup, prompting Chris to explain the template settings and member permissions for managing notification frequency. Chris also addresses the need to fine-tune the notification system and invites others to take the lead in understanding the help section."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-os6S3LK"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-8uOoIHE"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Waybook PM Manual Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Xtg32ZA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher gives a detailed overview of the PM manual in Waybook, highlighting the first chapter \"Build for PMs\" and the document containing member settings and notifications for different groups. He also covers the basic settings for files, permissions for each file in the tree, and the advanced settings such as package numbers and turning on\/off settings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7U5S11V"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Notifications and notification groups"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Wieootk"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"File Settings and Vendor\/Client Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LfqLsUp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a discussion on the necessity of configuring file settings before adding vendors or clients, stressing the need for consultation with designated team members. He also explains the advantages of adding vendors and the importance of implementing strict security measures to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive data. Justin Tumlinson raises concerns about the potential risks and challenges of integrating vendors into the system."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qh1UEFo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Invoice Approval Process and Impact on Financial Reports"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-wPFKVbp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jeremy Miles and Justin Tumlinson discussed the significance of approving invoices accurately in PIVO and PVOP, emphasizing the impact on financial reports and cost recognition in CE. They highlighted the need to hold invoices only for valid reasons and to ensure proper association with purchase orders to avoid discrepancies in cost reporting. Additionally, they mentioned the importance of reconciling purchase orders and rejecting invoices when necessary to maintain accurate financial records."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-dhwOSfT"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Invoice approval process in PIVO"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-GCKcf2i"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-Mp3PDeu"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8evdshL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting began with Chris Durocher presenting an overview of the Build process, emphasizing the need for everyone to review and adjust project settings to ensure consistency. He discussed project permissions and roles, outlining the specific roles of project administrators, BIM cart, field foreman, and site superintendent. Chris also delved into the intricacies of role-based notifications and group settings, emphasizing the meticulous approach taken to enable essential settings for all members."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-YwiX5tF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"He initiated a discussion on reducing nuisance notifications and avoiding overwhelming the team with excessive information. Chris also thoroughly explained the contents of the PM manual in Waybook, focusing on the first chapter \"Build for PMs\" and the document containing member settings and notifications for different groups. He guided the team through the process of setting up file settings and emphasized the need to consult with specific team members before adding vendors or clients."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-XyyHsFo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jeremy Miles and Justin Tumlinson led a discussion on the critical nature of correctly approving invoices in PIVO and PVOP, emphasizing the substantial impact on financial reports and cost recognition in CE. They stressed the importance of holding invoices only for valid reasons and ensuring proper association with purchase orders to prevent discrepancies in cost reporting. Furthermore, they highlighted the significance of reconciling purchase orders and rejecting invoices when necessary to maintain accurate financial records. The speakers also mentioned the availability of instructions in Waybook and offered assistance for navigating invoice approval processes to avoid skewing reports and budget discrepancies."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sBlUEq7"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-iWDHC8P"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Key Questions:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-stEg6AD"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* How do we ensure that all project settings are correct in Build?"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4UjloXK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* What is the process for approving invoices in PIVO?"}]}]}

steps.name: 6/25/24 - Sheets
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-lFOfitP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5Lqe5Tr"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/R1RW4FafOn4\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"76f3579092d956d00306b16064a2a316db0e19a421a87f6193d66fea8c8a7fc3","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-zetmPkr"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-TG2VxUi"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-VMbvYdd"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting began with Chris Durocher highlighting the positive feedback received on the bite-sized training materials and emphasizing the need for everyone to update their members, files, and notifications before delving into the new topic of sheets. He provided detailed guidance on creating collections, managing versions, and organizing disciplines, stressing the importance of understanding these aspects for efficient project management. The discussion also included valuable input from Vernon Long and Cody Henry on handling issuance dates and organizing disciplines effectively. Throughout the session, Chris emphasized the significance of building on previous training and ensuring that all team members are up to date with the necessary tasks to facilitate seamless project workflows."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BTH3ETo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting also covered permissions and role-based settings for sharing drawings, emphasizing the need to align settings with specific roles to avoid issues with accessing and editing drawings. Chris provided examples of how individual permissions can override roles and stressed the importance of verifying and adjusting settings to match the requirements in Waybook. The discussion also covered the impact of changing members without adjusting roles and the significance of this week's training in ensuring proper settings to avoid disruptions in accessing drawings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8bOcbd3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting also discussed the usage of Bluebeam for marking up drawings, highlighting the permanent nature of the markups and the difficulties encountered when using the tool. Chris recommended downloading drawings without markups for easier marking up and explained the various purposes for which markups are utilized, including RFIs and scheduling."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-N8WP6Z4"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-Jh3pXYv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SoqkOuN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Sheets, Collections, and Disciplines"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WLTerQ5"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a discussion on the necessity of updating members, files, and notifications, stressing the importance of building on previous training. He delves into the intricacies of sheets, collections, and disciplines, offering insights on creating collections, managing versions, and organizing disciplines to streamline project workflows. The session also includes valuable input from Vernon Long and Cody Henry on handling issuance dates and organizing disciplines for effective project management."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ftmiVrv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Collections and their use"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Q5s8sB0"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Permissions and Role-Based Settings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cKxss03"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a detailed discussion on setting permissions and roles for sharing drawings, emphasizing the importance of aligning settings with specific roles. He provides examples of how individual permissions can override roles and highlights the need to verify and adjust settings to ensure they match the requirements in Waybook."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WKHMGQf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Overview of Build Features"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-r5BKcg4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher delivers a rapid walkthrough of the features in Build, highlighting the process of adding sheets to version sets, utilizing tools for editing sheet numbers, and managing titles and tags for efficient page retrieval. He also mentions the publish log, viewing specific sheets for a version, and the limitations of the markup tool."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hqHlxt7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Filtering and organizing drawings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1bsqrQ6"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Strategies for Managing Multiple Buildings' Drawings and Specifications"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7HHYte8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill raises the challenge of managing drawings and specifications for multiple buildings within the same project. Chris Durocher suggests organizing specifications into separate folders and discusses potential future improvements in the specifications tool. The conversation also touches on the possibility of using version control for specifications."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vAzxjPh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Specifications and their management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-eVK2ypB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Bluebeam Markups and Usage"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Wviik6V"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides insights into the use of Bluebeam for marking up drawings, emphasizing the permanence of the markups and the challenges of using the tool. He suggests downloading drawings without markups for easier marking up and discusses the different purposes for which markups are used, such as RFIs and scheduling. Additionally, he mentions the ease of use for the iPad compared to the web-based version."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uiJlbS4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Utilizing Collections for Organizing Drawings and Sheets"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uYN7ulN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher shares insights on using collections in EDB to organize drawings, emphasizing the separation of non-contract and shop drawings. He explains the process of customizing the order of drawings and sheets within collections to facilitate easier access for field personnel. Additionally, Tyler Krenek highlights the ability to tag different collection sheets onto contract drawings and demonstrates how to link and jump to specific sheets within the collections."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zqXFdHT"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Markups and their management"}]}]}

steps.name: 7/3/24 - Locations
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-ZpnpZYo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CG8lTXb"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/VFfK8xbtzUw\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"9f13d494871fd84415aa2712890f79c7be1f16803ddcb8636f30aa548b754bea","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-9NGn5BB"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-PvD7egs"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hrM3prN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting covered several topics related to project management. Chris Durocher led discussions on the functionality of Read AI, training module settings, and the implementation of a system for setting up project locations to facilitate the reporting of field issues. He emphasized the importance of communication and efficiency in these processes."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2zP7jSh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The team also discussed safety concerns related to working at heights. Overall, the meeting aimed to streamline project management processes and ensure safety protocols were followed."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-eWAz5jK"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-LhmbUMp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pz7tqtZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Read AI and Training Module Settings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DFGxS0q"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a discussion on Read AI, seeking input from the team. Terri Lyon and Cody Henry express their satisfaction with Read AI's pre-meeting reports. Additionally, Chris Durocher addresses training module settings and provides guidance on resolving issues related to visibility of forms and sheets for field personnel."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sEsFVzo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Setting up and using locations in Waybook"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-VgAx2YH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Setting Up Project Locations and Linking Drawings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DoWWTvh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed explanation of the tiered structure for setting up project locations and linking drawings. He emphasizes the process of uploading locations using a template and demonstrates the import process. The discussion also highlights the importance of requesting and obtaining project location information early in the project timeline to streamline the setup process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-eByolqx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Setting Up Locations for Field Issues"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-z1NK0SZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher discusses the practical aspects of setting up locations for field issues, demonstrating how the process allows field personnel to easily mark and report issues on drawings using specific locations. He highlights the benefits of this method, such as streamlining the issue tracking process and providing detailed information for the general contractor. Durocher also explains how the system automatically generates a report with a table of contents, drawing references, and issue details."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jIRvLrw"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Uploading and linking drawings to locations"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-E6B8Dbd"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Using locations for marking issues in the field"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vlSn1UW"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Uploading Process and Safety Concerns"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-G5vRSNM"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides detailed instructions for the uploading process, highlighting the use of the correct template and stair-stepping method. The estimated time for the process is discussed, and safety concerns related to working at heights are raised by Jeremy Miles and Cody Henry, emphasizing the importance of paying attention to safety protocols."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Y90E0yN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Communication and collaboration within the team"}]}]}

steps.name: 7/16/24 - Forms
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-zjV5LVy"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JLmJAbr"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/vuykZkYZYOs\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"665496db9d8d949e69b0c725e65d0fe2b01f1fee56948bb466ba5ddb036a5302","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-MG3ZFAo"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-rTONNNV"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-q0yMvQi"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting covered several topics related to project management, including form settings and management, adjusting form permissions, Waybook forms functionality, exporting reports, contact groups for streamlined communication, correspondence module and email management, and obtaining vendor submittals and managing bulk orders for commodities. Chris Durocher led most of the discussions, emphasizing the need for attention to detail and adherence to the outlined procedures. The team engaged in detailed conversations, sharing experiences and tips for creating and utilizing various tools effectively to enhance communication and project management."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-VpUowtC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher led a detailed discussion on form settings and management, emphasizing the PM side of how things work. He explained the process of updating settings for forms and demonstrated how to do this for everyone, highlighting the frustration of different settings for each module in ACC. Chris also mentioned the collaboration with Nicky Arellano from the GS side to review the settings and the use of Waybook for reference. The conversation also delved into the process of setting up forms, the role of Sandra in loading company-approved forms, and the need for everyone to update their current projects to align with the new settings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uLznWWl"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The team also discussed the importance of contact groups for streamlined communication, emphasizing the advantages of this approach. Chris Durocher provided further insights into the differences between a group and a contact list, underlining the importance of using contact lists to avoid bounce-back emails and network issues. The group engaged in a detailed conversation about the practical implementation of contact groups in Outlook, sharing experiences and tips for creating and utilizing contact groups effectively to enhance communication within the network."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8XuF4kg"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Finally, the team discussed the process of obtaining vendor submittals and managing bulk orders for commodities. They explored the idea of requesting submittals from vendors for each job instead of maintaining a library, and the challenges of keeping the submittals up to date due to changing part numbers and manufacturers. The conversation also delved into the role of the purchasing department in maintaining up-to-date submittals for bulk orders, as well as the importance of communicating with the purchasing assistant to ensure the accuracy of bulk orders."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-rzErqsd"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-VBneqAb"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8X32Zyb"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Discussion on Form Settings and Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HV31pII"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Chris Durocher discusses the topic of forms, specifically focusing on the settings and management aspects. He explains the process of updating settings for forms and demonstrates how to do this for everyone, emphasizing the frustration of different settings for each module in ACC. He also mentions the collaboration with Nicky Arellano from the GS side to review the settings and the use of Waybook for reference."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-RTSjAoY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Forms and Settings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-TVfHsIN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Adjusting Form Permissions"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-N5b1TJi"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Chris Durocher leads a discussion on adjusting form permissions to ensure that more individuals can edit forms as required. He outlines the specific roles, such as project manager, site superintendent, and executive, that should have access to different forms and explains the settings that need to be updated for each role. The conversation also touches on the process of setting up forms and the need for everyone to update their current projects to align with the new settings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-9sgsh9i"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Waybook Forms Functionality"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-arEulKe"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Chris Durocher provided a detailed overview of the Waybook forms functionality, explaining the different perspectives of project managers and field workers when creating and processing forms. He stressed the significance of filling out form details to facilitate filtering and demonstrated the options to add references and notes to forms, despite the limitations of using PDF forms."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ga7QKvf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Exporting Reports and Customizing Formats"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-wENq3Io"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Chris Durocher provides a detailed demonstration of exporting reports in PDF or Excel format, showcasing the customization options and sharing capabilities. He stresses the significance of understanding the export process and offers guidance on navigating the potential pitfalls, while also highlighting the availability of detailed help resources for further assistance."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-St4ZaNp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Forms and Settings Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-RCAyiAC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Chris Durocher provides guidance on completing settings for each job, highlighting the significance of forms, templates, and commissioning. He underscores the necessity of a thorough review by designated team members before adding customers or vendors to a project, and urges all participants to take the instructions seriously as they are applicable to every project. Vernon Long reinforces the importance of adhering to the discussed practices for effective project management."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-gZgfgNS"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Templates and Settings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4L35KwI"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Utilizing Contact Groups for Efficient Communication"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pUTIGpm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Vernon Long introduces the concept of creating contact groups for effective communication and shares his successful experience with it. Chris Durocher elaborates on the distinction between a group and a contact list, highlighting the benefits of using a contact list to prevent bounce-back emails and network-related problems. The team discusses the practical steps for creating and utilizing contact groups in Outlook for improved communication within the network."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-FA56Qd2"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Discussion on Correspondence Module and Email Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pWAXqyU"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Chris, Joe, and Vernon engage in a discussion about the correspondence module and email management. Joe shares a method for automatically sending emails by adding the build project's email address to the contact list. The group explores the advantages of using the correspondence module for efficient email distribution and the importance of centralizing communication for future project management."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WiWQURG"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Contact Groups in Outlook"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WH1el1X"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Training and Project Management Discussion"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sHsTQ12"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Vernon proposes collecting ideas via email, Chris discusses upcoming trainings on project management topics, and Joe expresses frustration with the submittal process, hoping for improvements in the future."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-oU2h3Kh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Submittals and Cut Sheets"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bwq1t1V"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Discussion on Vendor Submittals and Bulk Orders"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-msuRV4u"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":" Chris, Vernon, and Joe exchange ideas on the process of obtaining vendor submittals and managing bulk orders for commodities. They discuss the possibility of requesting submittals from vendors for each job instead of maintaining a library, and the challenges of keeping the submittals up to date due to changing part numbers and manufacturers. The conversation also touches on the role of the purchasing department in maintaining up-to-date submittals for bulk orders. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-lz5xxg4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Bulk Buyouts and Purchasing"}]}]}

steps.name: 7/23/24 - Issues
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-GGKaZy8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jom7hX0"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/jVSDtHHl3LE\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"50af88494e42359af3e7350dde441a3541691c70d7f4d608e132841a5724ccac","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-vkClNi8"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-xWQCvlA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-umTRBTB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting began with Chris Durocher introducing the issues module in Build, which he believes should have been named \"lists\" due to its extensive list-keeping capabilities. The module provides flexibility and potential for creating various types of lists, and Terri Lyon recommends starting with schedule impacts. The discussion delves into the module's settings, including types, custom fields, permissions, root causes, templates, and statuses."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-rAx12DE"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a comprehensive discussion on the implementation of field walk templates for job sites, highlighting their efficiency in streamlining documentation processes. He also covers the functionality of the issues log and report generation within the system, emphasizing the system's robust capabilities and its potential for managing diverse tasks and assignments."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6v7ctCT"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The team also discussed the critical importance of saving job-related correspondence and documents on the server, citing specific examples of the time-consuming consequences of disorganized filing. Jeremy Miles underscores the significance of utilizing historical data and templates for subcontracts, emphasizing the time-saving benefits it offers. The team engages in a detailed discussion about the necessity of saving correspondence related to release dates and RFIs in the QMS log. The meeting concludes with Chris assigning tasks and updating the team on next steps."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HuRMNX8"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-bv3mpLL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WPFCqnv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Introduction to Issues Module in Build"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-0amNr81"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads the discussion on the issues module in Build, highlighting its functionality as a comprehensive list-keeping tool. He showcases examples of different lists, including incomplete work, schedule impacts, and startup lists, and emphasizes the flexibility and potential for creating various types of lists within the module. Terri Lyon also shares insights on utilizing the module for different job-specific lists and encourages starting with schedule impacts."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Dpm0rgp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Issues Module in Build"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-QwjerHy"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Custom Fields and Permissions"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-FZbdymr"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Lists and Templates"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-3uANYaL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Field Walk Template and Completion List"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4f8tMl8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher presents the concept of field walk templates, emphasizing their practicality for job site documentation. He provides a detailed walkthrough of how to create and utilize these templates, including the benefits of pre-populated fields and easy photo documentation. Additionally, he showcases the system's functionality for efficiently managing and marking completion statuses for multiple items."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-wE6e45q"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Issues Log and Report Generation"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5WILZq8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed walkthrough of the issues log and report generation process, highlighting the filtering and exporting options available. He demonstrates the issue summary report format and discusses the archival of completed items. Additionally, he explains the scheduling and distribution of reports to project members and external stakeholders."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qwAT0dL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Project Constraints"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pjrITTa"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Importance of Proper Document Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sU1Jsxu"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jeremy passionately stresses the necessity of saving essential job files on the server, citing specific examples of the time-consuming consequences of disorganized filing. Chris echoes this sentiment, emphasizing that emails are not a storage spot and urging prompt filing in the appropriate system. The conversation also delves into technical challenges related to syncing files across different platforms, with plans to address these issues."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-56IYW6I"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Document Management and Filing System"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-yTKdYnm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Utilizing Historical Data and Templates for Subcontracts"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-0YYNsri"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jeremy Miles stresses the value of leveraging historical data and templates for subcontracts, highlighting the efficiency it brings to the process. He advises team members to seek existing examples from PXs and PMs and emphasizes the willingness of the team to provide support. Chris Durocher reinforces the use of project management templates and offers assistance for any queries or uncertainties related to subcontracts."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-A4veLeo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Organizing and Saving Correspondence"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cp38gPK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris, Joe, and Jeremy discuss the importance of saving correspondence related to release dates and RFIs in the QMS log. They debate the best practices for organizing and saving emails, with Jeremy advocating for saving emails in a specific folder for easy access, while Chris and Joe share their different approaches. The team also discusses the challenges of organizing emails and the need for detailed subject lines to facilitate search and retrieval."}]}]}

steps.name: 7/30/24 - Assets
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-oaeRCJO"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WxiTQIX"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/5H__ti5mmiA\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"f9690a3fb7ead8510e94b20a3a8a09d86a0617ad7d310846518f9f5243aaea37","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-hCmiBu8"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-d7RKG7c"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-t0EoVmc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HP4s5jF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The team discussed technical updates on the tunnel connection and telecom, as well as the progress of the electrical tunnel and telecom installation. They also addressed the need for additional resources and the submission of drawings for approval. The team provided detailed updates on the completion of various tasks, such as setting up spider boxes and cranes, and the need for additional fixtures and temporary lighting for the crane. Chris Durocher led a training session on utilizing the meeting modules, covering the creation and management of open and closed items, sharing meeting minutes, and setting up follow-ups."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-isCLFcU"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-GlpeTKf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-E0ix76x"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Introductions and Technical Updates"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-G5jlYJn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher introduces Jeff Krim as the BIM manager and Mariano as the site superintendent. They discuss waiting for Nick Ariano and provide technical updates on the tunnel connection and telecom, including the status of the drawings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LMp3d8n"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* BIM modeling and shop drawings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ONCabW8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Project Updates and Coordination"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Mgopsup"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Mariano, Chris, and Jeff provide updates on the project's progress, including sign-offs for different levels and the need for additional resources. They discuss the submission of drawings for approval and coordination with other team members for updates and approvals. The conversation also touches on addressing issues with lighting protection and updating the QMS MISH need-by dates."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-N0h7XWe"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Level 0 and level 1 shop drawings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LhNu9r0"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Infrastructure and Equipment Updates"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-td3uT0z"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Mariano Ayala and Chris Durocher led a discussion on the progress of power infrastructure and equipment, covering tasks such as setting up spider boxes and cranes. They also addressed the need for additional fixtures and temporary lighting for the crane, as well as the progress of rental equipment and constraint logs. The team also discussed the coordination of work schedules and the status of inspections and fire command coordination."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DSWomPl"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Power infrastructure and lighting"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-QsFbhTe"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Training on Meeting Modules"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pRAOYOK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher conducts a training session on using the meeting modules, focusing on creating, managing, and sharing open and closed items, as well as setting up follow-ups. He also guides on creating and managing weekly meetings, assigning topics for discussion, and scheduling future meetings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-B3HJxJX"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Rental equipment and material lay down area"}]}]}

steps.name: 8/6/24 - Cost Management
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-8lktaVt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recordings"}]},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/bqX5CGTpkXc\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"33dad50405c40e1646342071f03b087ad8f8869199f572385610881f60ca9da7","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-47FUVXQ"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-3aeGXK9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZtDJ83h"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting was led by Chris Durocher, who covered a range of topics related to cost management, budgeting, change order management, and software tool functionalities. He emphasized the importance of assigning a person to every aspect of company setup and demonstrated the steps to set up a budget code format at different levels. Chris also provided a detailed walkthrough on creating and saving project views, stressing the importance of filtering by budget status and sharing views to streamline decision-making processes within the team. He also demonstrated the sorting and filtering options in Waco, focusing on the capability to organize and view RFIs and ASIs."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-FW9w4cb"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher also discussed the procedure for logging RFI responses in PCL, underscoring the PM's authority in deciding on cost implications. He emphasized the importance of documenting all potential change orders, even those not related to electrical work, to ensure accurate project records. Chris also led a comprehensive discussion on leveraging Build for budget management, covering the steps involved in creating views, adding cost items, and generating change orders. He meticulously explained the process of creating, editing, and submitting change orders, emphasizing the importance of setting the correct status and dates for effective tracking."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HAnd8IC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The discussion also revolved around the COR summary report, emphasizing its role in providing a consolidated view of CORs, OCOs, and PCOs across various projects. The team addressed the need for consistency in using statuses and the potential implications of the information presented in the reports. Additionally, there was a focus on the ability to include specific messages in the auto-generated client reports to provide clarity and prompt action from the clients. The meeting concluded with a discussion on the functionality of a module, emphasizing its limitations and providing a demonstration on manipulating certain elements."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-XaJXjWH"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-fLKL0iy"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Introduction to Cost Management in Build"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-a7LJIsh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides an overview of cost management in Build, highlighting its use for budgets, cost tracking, and change order logs. He explains the process of potential change orders, quotes, change order requests, and owner change orders, and emphasizes the importance of using the PCO, COR, and OCO logs for different types of documents and approvals."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bfdUTJZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Cost Management Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cHfNMTi"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Log Settings and Filters"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2451zVN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Company Setup and Budgeting"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-PGfqMsW"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed walkthrough of the company setup and budgeting process, highlighting the importance of assigning a person to every aspect of the company setup and demonstrating the steps to set up a budget code format at different levels. He also discusses the option to import a budget template and emphasizes the importance of setting up the PCO log with relevant columns for efficient tracking and decision-making."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CjqKbDk"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Budget Setup"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-axZCXQ7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Creating and Saving Project Views"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-q9zBTTZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher demonstrates the process of creating and saving project views, highlighting the significance of filtering by budget status and sharing views with the team. Cody Henry raises a question about setting up views in the template, and Chris explains the limitations and variations for each project, emphasizing the inability to set views in the template but suggesting the inclusion of a default budget change order code."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-nAU1WH7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Sorting and Filtering Options in Waco"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zYa3dUf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a demonstration of the sorting and filtering options in Waco, showcasing the ability to organize and view RFIs and ASIs. Vernon Long and Jeremy Miles participate in the discussion, raising questions about the limitations and potential enhancements of the sorting and filtering features. The conversation highlights the flexibility of the system in allowing users to customize their views based on specific criteria."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-f96MlCm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Logging RFI Responses in PCL"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uMQ2qps"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed walkthrough of the process for logging RFI responses in PCL, stressing the PM's role in determining cost. He illustrates how to set responses to void or open, and emphasizes the flexibility of making adjustments within the system. Jeremy Miles seeks clarification on reversing decisions, and Chris demonstrates the ease of making changes and the limitations on assigning numbers."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-xghjwSm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Change Orders and Document Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-S7qeRer"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jason Salas, Chris Durocher, and others deliberate on the necessity of recording all potential change orders, regardless of their electrical involvement, to maintain accurate project records. They address concerns about the time and effort required to input these changes and emphasize the need for transparency and accessibility for the field team.
