Chris also clarifies that the PCO log is for tracking contract value changes, not budget changes."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qxm9Cqc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Utilizing Build for Budget Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bV4KtgF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed walkthrough of using Build for budget management, showcasing the creation of views, addition of cost items, and generation of change orders. He highlights the significance of accurately matching COR folders on the server and AcuBit for change order logging, and also addresses the usage of Build for sending out documents to various trades."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-tpQshzd"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Managing Change Orders and Submissions"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uNTv1E4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed walkthrough of the change order process within the software tool, emphasizing the steps of creating, editing, and submitting change orders. He highlights the significance of setting the correct status and dates, and also addresses the ability to manually change the submitted date. Additionally, Cody Henry offers a suggestion for revising and resubmitting change orders, and Logan Sunvison seeks clarification on manually changing the submitted date."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-NhtHzVc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on COR Summary Report and Auto-Generated Client Reports"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6AN4DRy"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed explanation of the COR summary report and its significance for tracking CORs, OCOs, and PCOs across all jobs. He also discusses the scheduling and customization options for auto-generated client reports, highlighting the ability to tailor the information sent to clients based on their specific needs. The team also addresses the use of different statuses and the need for consistency in reporting."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-fSiq4AH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Reporting and Auto-Generation of Reports"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ByiyC5C"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on COR Log and Task Codes"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-epwHHbn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Nick Arellano raises the issue of needing descriptions in the COR log for better tracking, while Cody Henry points out the need for sorting the log in ascending order. Justin Tumlinson suggests using specific codes for internal use, and Jeremy Miles proposes a clear format for entering task codes to ensure clarity and ease of use."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-snBWCPv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Module Functionality and User Controls"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ONDcoWL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a discussion on the functionality of a module, highlighting its limitations and demonstrating how to manipulate specific elements. He engages with other participants, addressing their questions and inviting input on other topics for discussion."}]}]}

steps.name: 8/14/2024 Submittal Log & AutoSpec
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-V3M9Zgc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/cuHin8smZTo\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"5d39ee84f6f9cb4c425fbbb6c27b1e6a2f90638fb5451b6b7b987fa210d24766","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-5NHFCRx"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-gQ0t0HB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-PeCU2ud"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting focused on the detailed process of setting up the submittals module, including project numbering methods, user access permissions, default manager and reviewer roles, and the inclusion of general contractors and site superintendents in the system. The team discussed the importance of checking file settings to ensure that only authorized individuals have access and emphasized the need to avoid having \"everyone\" listed. They also added project members and set permissions for the build system, including the site superintendent and Kelvin. The team discussed the process of submitting and reviewing documents, outlining the specific roles of team members such as Josh, Jack, and Chase in preparing, reviewing, and approving documents before submission to the client."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-UXpcMl3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The team also discussed the use of review templates to streamline the workflow and the importance of accurately updating data and the roles of different stakeholders in the process. They emphasized the critical role of clear and consistent titles in facilitating easy retrieval and categorization and delved into the specifics of creating a folder system for organizing files. The team also discussed the use of read AI for training videos and the need to spread knowledge to enable more people to assist with the process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-AnKlCuu"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Finally, the team encountered issues while trying to publish product data and shop drawings to Autodesk Build, seeking help from Chase Heffernan to troubleshoot the problem. They discussed default values, project settings, and the submittal process, ultimately resolving the issue after some adjustments. Chris Durocher concluded the meeting by offering support to the team and encouraging them to reach out for assistance if needed."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-veT1xd2"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-vCKz1fm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SDqoUtH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Setting Up Submittals Module"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-IVH4n1l"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jack and Chris review the process of setting up the submittals module, covering topics such as project numbering methods, user access, default manager and reviewer roles, and adding general contractors and site superintendents to the system. They also discuss typical responses from the engineer of record and the types of documents that can be included in the module."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2kvvhM3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Setting up Submittals Module"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6Y0imPh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Submittal Process Workflow"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BfC8VUp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Spec Section Numbering"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pNJ0mYv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Review Templates"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZfSjIYn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"File Settings and Permissions Review"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-KNqJUAq"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a discussion on the necessity of reviewing file settings and permissions to prevent unauthorized access. He stresses the significance of project admins being automatically placed at the top of the list and the need to limit access for general contractors and vendors. The conversation also touches on the implementation of new templates for jobs and the importance of conducting a safety double check."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Yap90HX"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Adding Project Members and Setting Permissions"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vtrQE1x"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher and Jack Spurlock go over the process of adding project members and setting permissions for the build system. They add Kat Rommel as a project member, discuss sending invitations, and setting review times for different members, including the site superintendent and Kelvin. They also save the workflow template with all the permissions and default values set."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-PLv1jA4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Creating Submittal Log"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-wNI720I"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher guides the team through the process of creating a submittal log, presenting both manual entry and the auto spec method. He encounters some technical issues with file uploads but eventually successfully generates spec sections using the AI-powered auto spec feature, which will streamline the submittal log creation process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-xcfcNMp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Smart Register"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5d71mi8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reviewing Smart Register and Filtering Submittals"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CurXt09"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher walks the team through the smart register, highlighting the need to filter and organize the numerous action items. He demonstrates how to use the register to group and delete duplicate submittals, and emphasizes the importance of letting the AI do the initial work and then verifying its accuracy."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vlzr3AA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Troubleshooting Autodesk Build Publishing"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DfwEaR4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher faces difficulties in publishing product data and shop drawings to Autodesk Build, prompting a troubleshooting discussion with Chase Heffernan. They delve into project settings, default values, and the submittal process, eventually resolving the issue by making adjustments and clarifying project roles."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-TtEDZla"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Review and Submission Process for Lighting Specifications"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ovKoljP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides guidance on revising section titles and assigning responsible contractors to move items out of draft status. He explains the submission and review process, detailing the involvement of team members such as Josh, Jack, and Chase in preparing, reviewing, and approving documents before submission to the client. He also touches on the use of bookmarks and cover pages in the submission process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jkqQMfn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Workflow Process and Status Updates"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DD9Ogen"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provides a detailed explanation of the workflow process and status updates, emphasizing the need for coordination and communication with team members. He highlights the roles of different stakeholders, such as general contractors and vendors, and stresses the importance of accurately updating data to ensure smooth progress. Additionally, he addresses the learning curve associated with different status categories and the significance of understanding their meanings."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bjln1jl"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittal Process and File Organization"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pg9RVt6"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher and Chase Heffernan provide detailed guidance on the submittal process, stressing the significance of clear titles and organized file systems. They discuss the importance of proper file naming and categorization, as well as the need for training more individuals to ensure widespread knowledge and efficiency in the process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6x1WGP2"}}]}

steps.name: 8/20/24 Desktop Connector & E Drive
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8B18zHy"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/Ui5Du3f-Y1o\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"6f3b3d40ff9d34b25457f28b2b2700ba2f459d3d35c55d3ead85a1b478b621c3","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-tHO6Bk9"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-705ciHY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vbLmhW5"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting focused on the implementation of desktop connector and the transition to a new build server. Chris Durocher demonstrated the purpose and benefits of desktop connector, guiding Dereck through the installation process and emphasizing the importance of checking for updates regularly. The team discussed the process of migrating files to the new server, emphasizing the need to transfer items one at a time and communicate progress to colleagues. They also addressed security restrictions and suggested workarounds for transferring specific files. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-85lyFYT"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Additionally, Chris led a thorough discussion to ensure that all team members had installed the Desktop Connector and VPN on their laptops. He emphasized the significance of mapping the E drive using the VPN for easier access to documents and provided detailed instructions on how to request support for installation. Throughout the discussion, he addressed individual team members, encouraged prompt action, and answered questions to ensure everyone understood the process. The meeting concluded with a reminder to get the installation done for improved efficiency and workflow."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-d75ySNa"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-85CXc98"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Introduction to Desktop Connector"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vOuv2KD"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a session on desktop connector, showcasing its functionality and syncing capabilities between the laptop and the web. He highlights the advantages of using desktop connector and the company's shift towards working solely from the build files. Eric Middaugh and Vernon Long provide additional insights on file deletion and retrieval within the system."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-x8ifsdv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Accessing and Managing Projects in Autodesk Docs"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2eTL8YZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Transition to Build Server"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LwsVttL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vernon Long and Chris Durocher explain the transition plan to the build server, highlighting the research and approval from the board members. They stress the importance of no longer working on the current server once the transition is complete and address concerns about data syncing and access. The plan involves moving projects to build one at a time and labeling folders as \"build only.\""}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-91HH5mm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Transitioning from Server to Build"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-W9Cn6Od"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Installing Software on Desktop"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qpQiok5"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads Dereck through the installation process, emphasizing the importance of sharing the correct screen and following each step carefully. The meeting also includes discussions about the participation of other team members, the recording of the session for online reference, and the potential distribution of the software to site superintendents."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-E6rOnky"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Installation and Setup Process"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sAgUkga"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Server Migration and File Transfer Planning"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-lBqOPku"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris, Vernon, and Dereck strategize the migration of files to a new server, advising a cautious, one-at-a-time approach and clear communication with colleagues. They also touch on security restrictions and propose workarounds for transferring specific files, emphasizing the importance of careful handling, especially for folders with large amounts of data."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-35Mrin6"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Instructions on Using Autodesk Desktop Connector"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-aXAhE5h"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher guides Dereck through the process of using Autodesk Desktop Connector, showing him how to add and remove projects, check for updates, and install the latest version. He stresses the need to check for updates regularly and mentions that the link to the software is available in Weibook under Autodesk Desktop Connector. Additionally, he explains that the software can be accessed through the gray D icon."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vc2l7Xg"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Installation of Desktop Connector and VPN"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-fPCAnix"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher leads a discussion to ensure team members have installed the Desktop Connector and VPN on their laptops. He emphasizes the importance of mapping the E drive using the VPN for easier access to documents and provides instructions on how to request support for installation. Additionally, he addresses questions from team members and encourages everyone to get the installation done for smoother workflow."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-PtLTSFU"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* E Drive Mapping and VPN Access"}]}]}

steps.name: 9/10/24 ChatGPT
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-AUipC5x"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/fbXFI4JH0m0\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"e8e14a4d065b717395153a40a79cc4b0f22929bfd278c65c3123131ef1c55970","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-y5wXBwM"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-GLu9rZ6"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-mOQ0jqK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7LgpBsK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting began with a discussion about the use of Fireflies AI notetaker and the importance of wearing PPE and reporting safety issues. The team then discussed transitioning from formal Microsoft Groups to using contact groups in Outlook for project communication to streamline communication and improve the effectiveness of email correspondence within the project teams. Joe Hill introduced ChatGPT to the team, showcasing its capabilities, including answering questions and performing tasks. He also presented a demonstration of how AI technology can be leveraged to streamline the drafting of RFIs, showcasing its ability to clean up, format, and provide technical information with minimal manual input."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-wk0OW6M"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill also provided a detailed account of his use of AI for drafting project management correspondence, outlining the process of inputting information and refining the drafts generated by the AI tool. He highlighted the time-saving advantages and the interactive nature of the AI, which allowed him to coach the tool to produce a final version that met his requirements. Additionally, he discussed the AI's ability to mine content from the internet and adapt language tone based on his feedback, ultimately leading to the successful creation of a final draft for his correspondence."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vW5NxZP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The team also discussed the usage and limitations of ChatGPT, exploring its capabilities in processing data and providing quick answers to complex questions. They acknowledged the need for caution and verification of ChatGPT's outputs, and Joe Hill shared his plans to explore using ChatGPT for updating panel schedules and building material packages. The discussion concluded with a reminder to experiment with ChatGPT and share any interesting findings with the group."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WUt1tk5"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-M6QtfRy"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8zB8te5"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Arrival and Housekeeping"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-guR0XhK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher notes Joe Hill's absence and initiates housekeeping tasks while waiting for him. The team discusses the use of Fireflies AI notetaker and the importance of wearing PPE and reporting safety issues, as emphasized by Vernon Long."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-TucqTDr"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Use of Fireflies AI notetaker"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iQ9aORV"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Transitioning from Microsoft Groups to Contact Groups"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-OLhQB5x"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher and Vernon Long explain the shift from formal Microsoft Groups to contact groups in Outlook for project communication. They stress the importance of creating tailored contact groups for project teams and highlight the need to avoid overwhelming recipients with excessive emails. The change is driven by technical issues with Microsoft Groups and the desire to ensure that field personnel receive only relevant project information."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jxBk0So"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Use of Microsoft Groups for projects"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Eyq7Tct"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Introduction to ChatGPT"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vYNTLk9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher introduces Joe Hill, who presents an introduction to ChatGPT. Joe explains the different models available and demonstrates how to use ChatGPT for tasks like solving math problems and explaining concepts. He also discusses the mobile app feature for uploading images and provides examples of how he has used ChatGPT in the past."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pfnWfgC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Utilizing AI for Drafting RFIs"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HyibiBT"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill shares practical examples of using AI to draft RFIs, emphasizing the time-saving and accuracy benefits. He highlights the convenience of AI in handling technical details and providing quick explanations, enabling a seamless transition between different tasks without the need for extensive manual input or context switching."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sdleOrc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Using AI to streamline RFI writing process"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-17rQjNH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill demonstrates how he uses AI to streamline the RFI writing process by providing minimal input and letting the AI expand and add information from the uploaded PDF. He emphasizes the importance of simplicity and efficiency in the process, showing how the AI helps him refine and sanitize the RFIs before sending them out."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-x1Ns2qa"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Using AI to Draft Correspondence"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5rvc1QB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill shared his experience of using AI to draft correspondence for project management tasks, detailing how he input information and requested the AI to refine the drafts. He emphasized the efficiency and interactive capabilities of the AI tool, which allowed him to quickly produce a final version of the correspondence."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hSWCeIl"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill's Presentation on Chat GPT Model"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kqPhRxk"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill shared his experiences with training a Chat GPT model to perform tasks such as answering questions, creating digital versions of himself, and generating task lists from email content. He also discussed the potential of linking the model to specific websites and using plugins to improve email drafting and summarization, highlighting the model's ability to remember previous conversations and become smarter with increased usage."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jbV13g5"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Use of Maestro plugin for Outlook"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qRk97ry"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Utilizing AI for Drafting and Email Improvement"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CXEv9ox"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill explains how he uses AI to improve drafting and email writing, showcasing the tool's ability to create multiple drafts, clean up messy content, and assist in negotiations with contractors. He highlights the tool's role in addressing project delays and leveling the playing field for subcontractors, expressing enthusiasm for its potential impact on work efficiency and project positioning."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Mi4nLWs"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Electrical Prints and Fixture Types"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qCaq68i"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill demonstrates the use of a software to locate electrical prints and fixture types for a project, highlighting the efficiency of the tool in parsing through attachments and identifying fixtures. He also suggests the idea of using a project manager's name for verification study purposes, and discusses the process of loading up a verification study from Kelvin."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-3pe2VeG"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on ChatGPT Usage and Limitations"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-gHBa5aA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill, Logan Sunvison, Jason Salas, and others engage in a discussion about the usage of ChatGPT and its limitations, particularly in handling sensitive information and potential legal implications. They also explore the capabilities of ChatGPT in processing data and providing quick answers to complex questions, while acknowledging the need for caution and verification of its outputs."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pkSB8st"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Use of ChatGPT for creating project schedules and budgets"}]}]}

steps.name: 10/3/24 QMS Software
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Gyf95fI"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/1d8oU7oNgug\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"ed252dc7e04a9067a68e9d594d02818eafc59276a10cb1d462ac3df2666de584","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-7Is5lyZ"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sFEkpf2"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-V0v15Bn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-RBtU9Zn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting, led by Shaun Blumhoff, focused on the introduction of a new software system designed to enhance operational efficiency and streamline processes. Key features of the system were discussed, including its reporting capabilities that help identify potential issues and improve organization. Shaun and Justin Tumlinson elaborated on functionalities such as automated email notifications for vendors, user account setup, and the ability to send project reports with sorting and filtering options. These enhancements aim to provide a more organized approach to project management and facilitate better communication among team members."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SxRp27U"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun also presented updates to the Quality Management System (QMS) log template, which now includes new columns for vendor purchase order numbers, area and floor, and manufacturer representatives. These changes are intended to improve tracking and organization, particularly for distributors and gear items. The template will allow for easy downloading and uploading, with a focus on maintaining its structure to prevent errors. The discussion included the importance of using the app for adding new items and the need for vendors to input change orders directly into the template, ensuring clarity and consistency in data entry."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sA8OTPk"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The team engaged in a thorough discussion about the implications of adding new columns for change order references and vendor PO numbers, particularly in relation to the needs of project managers. They explored options to keep reports concise while meeting customer requirements and considered the impact of these changes on tracking and client visibility. Additionally, the conversation highlighted the importance of timely updates regarding material status and project timelines, emphasizing the need for proactive measures to address potential complications, such as missed deadlines and supply chain disruptions."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8AngxGY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting concluded with a focus on the QMS report format and its distribution, ensuring that the new system would be accessible to all relevant team members. Shaun provided details about the Excel format, which includes separate tabs for each QMS, and discussed the use of color coding for tracking progress. The team expressed appreciation for the ongoing efforts to implement the new system, recognizing its potential to improve communication and project management across the board."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vkONqJl"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-saDTlWe"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1OHQAMI"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Introduction to New Software System"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iYt03mR"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff and Justin Tumlinson discuss the features and functionalities of the new software system being implemented. They emphasize the system's ability to identify red flags on jobs and provide reporting to quickly pinpoint potential issues. Shaun also explains the process of setting up user accounts and the automated email notifications for vendors."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-QtHHbcB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Features and functionalities of the QMS software"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7N5b7T0"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Training on using the QMS software"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-YzJtmYF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Introduction to the new QMS software"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-3gfKPP9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Updates on QMS Log Template"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZQFeeT3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff provided a detailed overview of the updates to the QMS log template, highlighting the addition of new columns such as vendor PO number, area and floor, and manufacturer rep. These changes are intended to enhance organization and tracking, particularly for distributors and gear items. Additionally, the template will offer a download and upload feature for easy modifications."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZNjzkH1"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Template Usage and Change Orders"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-EPeHfvS"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff provides detailed instructions on using the template for making changes and warns about potential errors if the structure is modified. Justin Tumlinson emphasizes the use of change orders and clarifies that adding new items should be done through the app, not the spreadsheet. Vernon Long notes that the template is primarily for vendors to input change orders."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7t6yTRO"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Improving Reporting and Data Categorization"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DiFYLWE"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Justin Tumlinson emphasizes the importance of relevant and organized information in reports, while Shaun Blumhoff explains the use of item ID for warehouse management. The team discusses the necessity of including change order numbers and fixture types in the reporting system, addressing concerns about consistency and the need for custom columns."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2AJ8l1A"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Adding a New Column"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-R5nAqcm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vernon Long and Shaun Blumhoff lead a discussion on the necessity of adding a new column for change order reference and vendor PO number. They stress the importance of accommodating the needs of CED, a top project manager, while also considering the impact on other vendors. The team deliberates on the best approach to keep the report concise and customer-friendly."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-aWU4NQg"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Project Management Tool Features Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SWgilsL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff provided an overview of the updates in the project management tool, emphasizing the modifications in nomenclature and the functionality to add and view notes that are not visible to the customer. He also demonstrated the change log feature, which enables tracking of changes made to items in the tool, ensuring accountability and oversight."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-gtUg669"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Material Status and Project Issues"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BUPN5qj"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff, Justin Tumlinson, and Jeremy Miles address concerns about material status and project issues, focusing on the impact of release dates and due dates on the job site. They emphasize the need for proactive updates to prevent potential schedule disruptions and explore potential solutions to mitigate the impact of missed deadlines."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SXZQDKv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on QMS Log and Reporting"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JkOqOEs"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Justin, Shaun, and the team delve into the details of the QMS log and reporting process, highlighting the impact of transit time on release dates and material due on job site. They stress the need for accurate data entry and discuss the color coding system for tracking progress. Additionally, they address the role of vendors in updating release dates and the significance of identifying storage locations for materials."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JhGMTYK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Material Release and Port Issues"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1f4pBUA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff provides an update on material release and port issues, highlighting the need to maintain contingency plans due to potential supply chain disruptions. Justin Tumlinson and others express concerns about lead times and the impact of port issues on the supply chain, emphasizing the importance of proactive measures to mitigate potential risks."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-90Wvaki"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Program Management and Communication Process"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-blcToxv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Justin Tumlinson and Shaun Blumhoff discuss the role of Emily in managing the program, assigning vendors and project management teams, and communicating with GC contacts. They highlight the need to update the system and ensure that Sandra archives the old log once the jobs are in the new system. They also address the process of sending reports and ensuring that GCs receive them without any issues."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HfGC5Wy"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Communication and coordination with vendors, GCs, and internal team members"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HL9oSvU"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"QMS Report Format and Distribution"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-GnhRslJ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff explained the QMS report format, emphasizing that it will be an Excel file with separate tabs for each QMS. The team also discussed the use of colors and the distribution of the report to the project team, ensuring that it will be accessible to various team members."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-E2Mj8C0"}}]}

steps.name: 10/08/24 Procurement Process
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cG1wM7X"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/C1micivMbqI\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"9f1966ab980ca2783a55adcd3052d83c21b19137f7f7b7b3b9c55886e21c6353","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-WgWgUbG"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-lEtkK4t"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-paI08Ib"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting focused on the implementation of a new process for quoted material buyouts, led by Vernon Long, who emphasized the importance of completing procurement before the project management team takes over. He encouraged active participation from attendees, as some would be asked to read from shared materials. Justin Tumlinson highlighted the critical nature of timely purchase orders, noting that delays could significantly impact project timelines, especially given current equipment lead times. The Waybook was introduced as a uniform guide to streamline the process, detailing essential items and timelines."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JaNx6C0"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Long provided a comprehensive overview of the buyout process, underscoring the roles of Shaun Blumhoff and Shena in procurement and vendor negotiations. He stressed the need for project managers to stay informed and engaged to enhance efficiency and savings. Documentation was identified as a key component for ensuring transparency and accountability, with input from Tumlinson and Rolando Reyes clarifying responsibilities and next steps. The overarching goal was to streamline operations and foster collaboration among team members to avoid lapses in project execution."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-nx3NEz0"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The discussion also addressed specific challenges, such as delays in material procurement for two San Antonio jobs, with Tumlinson emphasizing the need for timely updates from Mark and Shena. Long suggested that project managers should actively engage with Mark to expedite responses from general contractors. The importance of Shena's involvement in the procurement process was reiterated, and Blumhoff proposed a meeting to clarify responsibilities regarding miscellaneous items. Additionally, Joe Hill raised concerns about tracking leftover materials, prompting suggestions for team members to create their own buyout lists to enhance organization."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-UYC4MBe"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Towards the end of the meeting, Long discussed the logistics of storage and delivery for the TE project, recommending coordination with project superintendents and foremen. He emphasized the need for direct shipping of materials to vendors for inspection and the efficient handling of large items by rigging companies. The conversation concluded with a focus on the critical nature of purchase order revisions, with participants sharing experiences on vendor communication and the importance of timely updates. The meeting underscored the team's commitment to optimizing costs, improving vendor relationships, and enhancing data management through new reporting systems like Power BI."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-U4x4IW0"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-DNCMI7H"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-l7klZ0L"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Quoted Material Buyouts Process Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7JgcIAP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vernon Long outlined the new quoted material buyouts process, stressing that it will be completed before the job is handed over to the project management team. Justin Tumlinson added that while the goal is to streamline this process, there may be instances where delays occur, necessitating involvement from the project team to ensure timely execution. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1lz7SNA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Quoted Material Buyout Process Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hiMbYU4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vernon Long outlined the responsibilities of the Director of Procurement, Shaun Blumhoff, and the importance of Shena's role in negotiating vendor terms and pricing. He stressed that project managers must be proactive in understanding the buyout process to facilitate smoother operations. Additionally, he mentioned the need for proper documentation and communication among team members to track purchases effectively."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8B9Hk9j"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Analysis of purchasing data and vendor performance."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5UBEcDU"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Coordination on Project Material Procurement"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6byYDmE"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Justin Tumlinson expressed concerns about delays in issuing purchase orders (POs) for project materials, particularly due to pending approvals for gear and fixtures. Vernon Long recommended that project managers coordinate with Mark to ensure timely responses from general contractors. The discussion also touched on the importance of involving Shena in the procurement process to leverage his knowledge of available materials and lead times."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZAW3v7S"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"QMS Organization and Material Buyouts"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-xdqOoP2"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Joe Hill expressed concerns about managing leftover materials and the buyout process, prompting Chris Durocher to recommend creating a personal buyout list for better tracking. Logan Sunvison sought clarification on the organization of the QMS, which led to Justin Tumlinson highlighting the importance of input from the project team and superintendents. Shaun Blumhoff mentioned ongoing training and meetings to ensure the QMS is structured effectively to meet project needs."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hJMq2dY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Project Schedule and Submittal Process"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BIhg1q2"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Justin Tumlinson highlighted the need for timely issuance of purchase orders and submittals, comparing it to previous projects like Cedar Park High School. He stressed the importance of understanding lead times and establishing a clear \"drop dead date\" for releasing materials to ensure alignment with the project schedule."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-eS4eXfD"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Material Order Review and Procurement Strategies"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7y4HQiB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Cody Henry discussed the project manager's role in reviewing material orders and coordinating with the DOP for any necessary price modifications before material release. Vernon Long highlighted the need to verify changes and credits with vendors, especially after receiving Kelvin's study. Shaun Blumhoff added that he aims to improve tracking of credits from vendors to ensure fair compensation."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-5cmQgqP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Storage and Delivery Coordination for TE Project"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-3VYnQJp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vernon Long discussed the necessity of reviewing storage options with the TE project superintendent and foreman, particularly regarding the use of connexes for staging fixtures. He advised that all materials should be shipped directly to the vendor for inspection and storage, while large items that cannot be stored should be coordinated with rigging companies for delivery. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-InSPJGc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Purchase Order Release and Verification Process"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-msFvJ0U"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vernon Long discussed the critical steps involved in releasing purchase orders, stressing the need for thorough verification of comments and responses from vendors. Justin Tumlinson pointed out that many teams fail to obtain confirmation from vendors regarding the incorporation of changes, which can lead to issues later. Jeremy Miles added that face-to-face meetings with vendors can help ensure understanding and compliance."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-VmzFDwm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Importance of Timely Purchase Orders and Submittals"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-mYkgS7j"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Purchase Order Revisions and Change Order Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-v4uGNZO"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The discussion highlighted the process for handling purchase order revisions, with Vernon Long and William Thornell detailing the responsibilities of the project manager in submitting requests. Cody Henry and Jeremy Miles shared their practices of involving vendors early in the process to expedite material delivery, while Justin Tumlinson stressed the need to update POs and subcontracts promptly after change orders are approved."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ecRyqlt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Tracking and Managing Purchase Order Revisions"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4fbOP1n"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Vendor Coordination and Cost Management Strategies"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CsiJ1DM"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff discussed the need for transparency with vendors when mistakes occur, proposing that they could negotiate to mitigate costs. Vernon Long reinforced this idea, urging the team to leverage Shena's expertise for buyouts and pricing strategies. Jack Spurlock raised a question about utilizing specific buyouts, which Vernon confirmed as a valuable approach."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-lTU3CUN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Progress on Data Management and Reporting Tools"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Dzx8OoY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Shaun Blumhoff outlined the progress being made in enhancing data management and reporting tools, noting that the transition is a learning curve but is yielding positive results. The new Power BI reports will enable users to view job expenditures and vendor information in real-time, streamlining the invoice approval process. Blumhoff also mentioned the importance of accurate data capture for effective project management."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-IBs60RG"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Development and implementation of Power BI reports for job tracking."}]}]}

steps.name: 10/16/24 Submittal Module Overview
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-wSei290"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ixDxy7Q"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/azO41ZC_SdE\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"8eae680adc35d5808e78affffce5fca75d3d290222c006447b6f586a9de3c7c7","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-oseK3Sz"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-DvzcM1q"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-VRX00DL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting, led by Chris Durocher, focused on enhancing the team's understanding and management of submittals and specifications within the project management system. Durocher emphasized the complexity of the submittal process, advocating for a structured approach that includes breaking down approved submittals into individual components for better organization. He highlighted the importance of incorporating all engineer comments to avoid miscommunication in the field and noted that the updated procedures are documented in the PM manual for reference. The discussion aimed to familiarize the team with the submittal module and improve overall document management practices."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ANnSJM7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Durocher provided a detailed overview of the functionalities of the spec management system, including the setup of auto specs and the creation of submittal logs. He explained the importance of managing items in draft status until they are ready for submission and encouraged users to enable the auto specs feature in their settings. The meeting also covered document filtering capabilities for managing project-related documents, with Durocher demonstrating how to filter and export documents based on various criteria. The session concluded with an invitation for questions, indicating a positive engagement from participants regarding the interface and functionalities discussed."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DI8N91z"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The conversation also addressed the workflow for handling submittals, with Durocher outlining the sequential process from product data submission to final approval. He stressed the need for clear communication among all parties involved, including the General Contractor and Project Manager, to ensure effective reviews and timely project progression. Participants shared their experiences and challenges with the workflow tool, highlighting a split in familiarity among team members. The meeting concluded with a commitment to refine the submittal process in future discussions, ensuring that all team members are aligned and equipped to utilize the tools effectively."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-NpxAjMJ"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-JWWAm3m"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-NHNnw6w"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittals Overview and Process Improvement"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Dag1eNE"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provided an overview of the submittal module, highlighting its robust features and the importance of breaking down approved submittals into individual components. Vernon Long added that all engineer comments must be incorporated into each panel board to ensure clarity for field personnel. The goal is to enhance document management and accessibility."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6UfVBmJ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Process for breaking down approved submittals into individual components."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kWtCBWF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Importance of including engineer comments in submittals."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kbvmJkV"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Spec Management and Submittal Log Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Rlim9Eh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provided an overview of the spec management system, explaining how to navigate its basic columns and access detailed information. He mentioned the use of auto specs to streamline the creation of spec sections and submittal logs. Durocher also outlined the process for creating items, either in draft status or as actual specs, and encouraged team members to explore importing from Excel for list development."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-AEKjazx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Document Filtering and Reporting Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zJLqJZL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher provided an overview of document filtering capabilities, showcasing how users can search for specific documents and track their status. He pointed out the significance of obtaining necessary responses from general contractors to proceed with project tasks. Additionally, he demonstrated the process of exporting filtered data into formats like Excel and PDF for client communication."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-L9Hkl5n"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittal Management Workflow Discussion"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-PxnxlEh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher outlined the workflow for managing submittals, highlighting the roles of the owner, architect, general contractor (GC), and subcontractors. He explained that the GC acts as a central figure, assigning tasks to subcontractors, who then return their submissions for approval from the architect. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zwHjSbC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Workflow management for submittals and the roles involved."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-OjdGEsF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittal Process Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-QZmerYl"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher explained the submittal process, highlighting the timeline from when product data is sent to vendors to the final approval stages. He noted that the process includes internal reviews to ensure compliance and the ability to track revisions if submissions are rejected. Durocher also mentioned the importance of maintaining a log of all items and their statuses."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-lJBaWaW"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Roll Call and Workflow Discussion"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JGBj23o"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher led a roll call to determine which participants had experience with a particular workflow tool. Joe Hill raised concerns about his understanding of the workflow, noting that he had been using some features but lacked clarity on the overall process. The roll call revealed a roughly even split between those familiar with the tool and those who were not."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-K1k74EP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittal Process Overview"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JRJL1TQ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher detailed the submittal process, starting with the creation of a submittal log and assigning tasks to responsible individuals. He clarified the workflow, which requires completing each step before moving to the next, and discussed the importance of setting permissions and response types for submittals. Durocher also mentioned the role of project managers and submittal managers in overseeing the process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-dtih3Lq"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Review Process Setup"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-nioEWvc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher outlined the structure of the review process for project submissions, highlighting the flexibility to add multiple review steps. He recommended having the PM review first, followed by the GC, to prevent any oversights. Joe Hill acknowledged the significance of including the GC in the review process to clarify the status of submissions."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-lTPqzBf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Workflow and Submittal Process Discussion"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-EKoEFI5"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher outlined the workflow for managing submittals, highlighting the advantages of the new build module over traditional spreadsheets. Nick Willis inquired about the standard procedure for sending approved submittals to vendors, and Vernon Long stressed the need to keep track of unapproved submissions to prevent issues in the field. The team discussed the importance of including notes with submittals to ensure clarity for the field."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-yOPAaPF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Auto Specs and Specifications Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-foiJ5rs"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher expressed enthusiasm for Auto Specs, emphasizing its efficiency in finding items for submittal logs. Cody Henry raised a concern about the inability to save markups within the program, which Vernon Long confirmed. Joe Hill shared an experience where he had to use a different viewer due to issues with duplicate spec sections, illustrating some limitations of Auto Specs."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Qtor1uK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Use of Auto Specs for managing specifications and submittals."}]}]}

steps.name: 10/24/24 Asset Management
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-kbxH27O"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2SqbMz9"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/sTXlhR4ocj0\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"a7c2e0dce95485a51127254a9f5a2b91d79d1eda319f3480d81ee15b96738ef4","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-O4CfoPS"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-fLTfkKc"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-S7WvTTH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting, led by Chris Durocher, centered on the asset management system essential for tracking commissioning items like electrical panels and mechanical units. Durocher introduced Greg and Austin, who will manage the asset list and ensure its timely updates in the field. He stressed the necessity of having the QAQC team accurately fill out commissioning statuses and noted that only project managers would have administrative rights to prevent unauthorized changes."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sHfHl5S"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Cody Henry raised concerns about discrepancies in status colors, underscoring the need for adherence to established asset management protocols. Durocher also highlighted the urgency of uploading locations to aid field workers in linking assets, with Vernon Long suggesting that room numbers should be sourced from architectural plans to avoid manual errors."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Mk0aMa2"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The discussion transitioned to project management issues, where Durocher addressed various questions from team members, including difficulties with member setups and asset documentation. He clarified that documenting assets for projects nearing completion was unnecessary for those already submitted for closeout. Rolando Reyes brought up challenges with the submittal process, particularly regarding attachments, prompting Durocher to outline the workflow for managing submittals effectively."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bsXCHvS"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting also covered the auto specs program, with discussions on best practices for managing specifications and the importance of filtering relevant submittals. Overall, the team recognized the need for improved tools and practices to enhance workflow efficiency, with Durocher indicating plans to develop a policy for creating PDFs to streamline processes for the field."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jgCwwuc"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-tsCUEJY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6v8K3tK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Asset Management and Commissioning Updates"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-r5xE33P"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher discussed the asset management system, highlighting its role in tracking various electrical and mechanical items that require commissioning. He mentioned that Greg and Austin are tasked with creating and maintaining the asset list, while also stressing the need for field personnel to manage and update asset statuses accurately. Durocher pointed out that only project managers will have administrative access to settings to prevent unauthorized changes."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-AT5VJjY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* The process of updating and managing assets in the system."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-QzB4YWK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Overview of asset management and its importance in commissioning."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-yfqLFM7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Urgent Updates on Asset Management and Location Settings"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-lk5iutP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher discussed the necessity of having locations uploaded for projects to ensure field workers can link their assets correctly. He highlighted the urgency of this task, especially as teams are actively engaged in multiple projects. Vernon Long added that room numbers should not be manually entered, as they are typically available in architectural plans."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-DnLCX6Y"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Importance of maintaining accurate status settings and locations for assets."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-YiBXpsk"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Project Management Questions and Updates"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-3HXPksN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher facilitated a discussion on project management, responding to inquiries from team members. Cody Henry experienced issues adding a member to a project, which Durocher clarified was due to a spelling error. Nick Willis sought guidance on whether to document assets and locations for projects in the closeout phase, to which Durocher advised that it was not necessary for completed projects but important for new ones."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zyaNjnA"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Urgency in completing asset and location setups for ongoing projects."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kHuGH04"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Technical Difficulties and Participant Check-ins"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-mZPncqP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher facilitated the meeting by checking in with attendees and addressing technical difficulties, particularly with Rolando's audio. He welcomed Brian and engaged with other participants, including Cody Henry and Logan Sunvison, who were discussing upcoming events."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-IJTIS2U"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittal Process and Workflow Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Sw4w7tS"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Rolando Reyes raised concerns about the submittal process, noting issues with attachments being linked rather than attached. Chris Durocher clarified the workflow, explaining how to set up the order of document reviews and the importance of managing attachments correctly. He emphasized the need to avoid linking references that could lead to confusion."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-n8wJRyw"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Auto Specs and Submittals Workflow Discussion"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-fMCrtrb"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The conversation focused on the functionality of the auto specs program, with Chris Durocher highlighting the importance of filtering specifications before uploading. Rolando Reyes shared his experience with the program crashing and the resulting complications. Vernon Long emphasized the need to select specific items to streamline the submittal process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BTkOh2A"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Discussion on Specification Testing and Document Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-fdqZweh"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher outlined the need for testing on Division 26 and how he plans to communicate this to his superintendent. Rolando Reyes shared insights on exporting specifications and the benefits of using the spec view for organization. Vernon Long cautioned against using the specification section for notes, highlighting its inability to accommodate modifications."}]}]}

steps.name: 10/29/24 Submittal Example
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-Cg8HaNS"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Video"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hb4ot3L"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/AMgLhwope9w\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"c508b03bf099a14e58d0878e5321cffc103e43a855f88423528b2b39a469f426","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-RaVT3QK"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZphmSIC"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-H3AqoCS"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-YJRTprp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iqVYldL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher led a comprehensive training session focused on the submittal process, specifically detailing how to create commodity and fixture package submittals using Chase as a case study. He provided a step-by-step demonstration, highlighting essential settings such as permissions and the role of the submittal manager. The training emphasized the importance of selecting the correct templates and types, as well as the necessity of including accurate email addresses for general contractors and vendors. Durocher also outlined the product data submission process, assigning a task to Trent with a seven-day deadline, and stressed the importance of updating submission dates to manage overdue items effectively. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-NsSLzuw"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The meeting further explored the assembly of lighting packages, with Durocher demonstrating the use of Bluebeam for document markup and compliance checks. He explained the review process, indicating a two-day assessment period before submission to the general contractor. Discussions included the need for manual setup of workflows and review templates, as well as the potential for bulk editing templates for drafts."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-AQXuk8e"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Nick Willis proposed improvements to the workflow for submitting commodity materials, while concerns were raised about resetting configurations for new projects. The session concluded with discussions on user access issues, particularly regarding the limitations of the iPad app compared to the browser, and a reminder for team members to seek assistance with any challenges they encounter."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-KpHxEQR"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-8CITAD1"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-2BINPEv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittal Process Training"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cjdtelL"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"In a training session, Chris Durocher demonstrated the process of creating submittals, specifically focusing on raceways and lighting fixtures. He highlighted key settings, including permissions and the assignment of contractors, while using Chase as a practical example. The session aimed to clarify the steps involved in submittals, including the selection of templates and types."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-NTRBlyC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Creation of submittals for raceways and lighting fixtures."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-tLxB2D1"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Use of templates and default values in the submittal process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-K0Piluv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Product Data Submission Process"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-L6R7Gr1"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher explained the procedure for handling product data submissions, assigning Trent a task due in seven days. He highlighted the need to keep track of submission dates, noting that if a submission is overdue, it should be marked accordingly. Durocher also demonstrated how to correct any mistakes in the submitted data."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Nbgq1Cd"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Lighting Package Preparation and Review Process"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Mf7uUJq"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher outlined the steps for creating a lighting package, including marking up specifications and combining documents using Bluebeam. He noted the importance of organizing files correctly and ensuring that the specs are in the right order. Durocher also mentioned the review process, where he would verify the package before submission to the general contractor."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jfmQE06"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Submittal Workflow and Management"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cwQsD68"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher detailed the submittal management process, including how to organize files and communicate with clients. He clarified that workflows must be manually set up for each project and discussed the importance of having multiple review steps. Nick Willis raised a question about the workflow setup, which Durocher confirmed requires manual configuration."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bPXoeqs"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Issues related to mobile app functionality and user permissions."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sZMtDme"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Review process and compliance for submitted documents."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-GIP9bub"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Workflow and Reporting Enhancements Discussion"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-oTa5r2b"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Nick Willis suggested developing a workflow to enhance the process of submitting commodity materials to the General Contractor. Chris Durocher highlighted the importance of standardizing practices, noting past issues with excessive notifications when using the software. He also mentioned that Autodesk is considering a feature to allow direct emailing from the platform, while discussing the possibility of adding logos to project cover pages."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cFZsEh0"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"iPad Issue Management and Permissions"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-yULpyRD"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jason Salas highlighted a limitation when using the iPad to manage issues, specifically the inability to change titles. Nick Arellano explained that this is due to differences in functionality between the app and the browser, and he offered to guide Jason through the process. Cody Henry and Joe Hill also contributed by discussing their experiences with the app versus browser usage."}]}]}

steps.name: 11/12/24 COR Set Up Form
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-mjv70pK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-i5rE7np"}},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/qGjNGN154fU\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"303c5d5dc893279c81e47ebb290d420a8d248b89cc525425dd33026ed3bd4a4a","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-1sdMtaW"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-4M9wXJh"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-AerJbNT"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Y481jSa"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chris Durocher conducted a training session on the new Change Order Setup Form, which aims to replace the outdated Excel spreadsheet method for managing change orders. The new form is designed to streamline the review process by summarizing essential information such as labor rates and general expenses, and it is to be utilized for all new projects, while existing change orders may still use the old format. The team was informed that further training on the change order process will be provided by Vernon upon his return from vacation. The session encouraged questions but was kept brief to honor participants' time."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SYITPG6"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-ya9PPJ8"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Chapters & Topics:"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6I5ZCFD"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Change Order Setup Training"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iOqwVwM"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jason Salas introduced the Change Order Setup Form, a new tool designed to simplify the change order process. This form consolidates necessary information, such as labor rates and general expenses, into a single document for easier review by Justin. The team is encouraged to use this form for all new projects moving forward, while existing change orders can remain as they are."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-NITAF7O"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Introduction of the new Change Order Setup Form."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-O3XbNDT"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Accessibility of the Change Order Setup Form in Waybook and project management templates."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zltToCg"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Importance of labor rates and general expenses in the change order process."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WtX4Ay6"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"* Process for filling out and submitting the Change Order Setup Form."}]}]}

steps.name: 3/4/25 Build Orientation
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":2,"id":"pm-8Fvt4a9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Recording"}]},{"type":"embed_frame","attrs":{"srcdoc":"<iframe src=\"https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/embed\/Y3W-ZU9UpRQ\" allowfullscreen style=\"width:100%;height:100vh\" frameborder=\"0\"><\/iframe>","signature":"79f93ae0608e889dd1c24559117ede5f2898ce17b31d8866bac63ea2722fb55f","width":"100%","height":"","id":"pm-h5wpbQj"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-RFdafgE"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Overview","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1SQi6B9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Build Orientation meeting led by Chris Durocher provided an in-depth introduction to Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) and its various modules crucial for project management. Participants were guided through logging into ACC and navigating its functionalities, including Docs, Auto Specs, Build, Cost Management, and Insight. Key features discussed included the Sheets module for markup tools, the Files module\u2019s standard folder structure, and the Issues module for tracking project concerns. The training covered essential tools such as Forms, Photos, RFIs, Submittals, and Meetings, equipping attendees with skills for field use and project communication. Additionally, Chris explored the Assets and Reports modules, focusing on equipment tracking and project documentation. The session concluded with instructions on access management, emphasizing the importance of not altering project settings until all training is complete, while participants were given action items to update their contact information and calendar schedules for improved project coordination.","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hhl8Ko3"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hhl8Ko3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Notes","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zaw32iZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\ufe0f Introduction to Teams and Build (00:01 - Key segments included a demonstration of the change order log, an extensive walkthrough for setting up new change orders, and an introduction to the pricing process via Accubid, emphasizing the need for accurate documentation and budget creation. The discussion covered the project setup and global defaults to ensure proper labor rates, as well as the installation needs for new team members and available training resources. Action items were assigned to various participants to facilitate ongoing training and updates within the processes discussed.","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CGkJ0e3"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-3vRkH5p"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Notes","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1rW7Gnp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Change Order Process Overview (02:04 - Key topics included the necessary PDF and Excel exports from Accubid for budget adjustments, the use of pivot tables for data organization and analysis, and proper categorization of items through data scrubbing. Chris led the session, demonstrating how to break down labor hours, assign area and phase codes, and create informative pivot charts. Participants learned the importance of maintaining accurate data for labor and material tracking, as well as how to use the goal seek function to ensure profitability. Action items were assigned to Corey and Chris, who will address data issues and develop a training video for future reference.","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-UZhzXT7"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vT2oL2N"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Notes","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":[]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-9hfY9KC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\ufe0f Budget Building Basics (00:54 -
