steps.name: Project Budget
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3,"id":"pm-iWok8fC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Project Budget"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SGj1ihR"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The project budget will be set up before the project begins. The actual hours budgeted for installation of an activity code may vary from the estimate. This is because there may have been some Value Engineering (VE) added after the original estimate, or we may have found a better way to install materials than originally estimated. The lowest installed cost should be reviewed. For example: Changing from overhead installation of EMT to underground PVC, as PVC is usually faster to install, less hours may be budgeted to that code. The Super\/Foreman and Project Manager will coordinate the final labor budgets. Once these budgets are established the project Super\/Foreman will have knowledge of all activity codes and their budgeted hours to"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-AIwlwgg"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"allow for accurate planning of the project."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-M5xktNO"}}]}

steps.name: Activity Code System
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3,"id":"pm-BukfkLK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Activity Code System"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-GYSq9sX"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tumlinson Electric (TE) uses an activity code system to group hours in the project to allow for accurate tracking of progress. TE breaks down all activities on their projects into individual tasks. There is a standard activity code sheet which describes all activities and what they include. This may vary by project and is only a guide. Some high-rise projects may split activity codes down into floors, or large projects may be zoned. All activities to be tracked will be finalized prior to the Start Up Meeting."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kSq3iUX"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-s6a77Qx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If an activity code is not opened for a job, no time can be charged to it. For example, labor to install 1\u201d or smaller EMT conduit is listed as 000.0100 code. All labor used to install 1\u201d or smaller branch conduit will be charged to the 000.0100 labor code. Accurate reporting of activities is encouraged as future bids will use previous project historical data as a benchmark. Accuracy is needed, not budgets that look good to others. The Super\/Foremen are the key to the success or failure of this system."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-H4F0Ygl"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Uv5aUce"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"You will find a complete activity code breakdown for your reference TE Document Templates of Waybook. This should be used for reference purposes only, as the Project Manager will provide you with a job specific activity code breakdown. You will only use the activity codes opened by your Project Manager for your project. Remember, if you use activity codes that have not been opened you will cause invalid time on your time cards. Invalid time will cause your Superintendent, Project Manager, Cost Accounting, and yourself additional time to research the activity code that should have been used. When in doubt, or if budgets are not available, use standard activity codes. You should have a printout of all activity codes and their description before starting work."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-eKRjpoI"}}]}

steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uVdkYtU"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-L1UAdK3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tumlinson Electric \u2013 Best Practices Submittals Policy","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-OPZLDZ7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Purpose","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Kkw30No"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Kkw30No"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This policy ensures consistent, proactive, and compliant submittal practices across all projects. By adhering to Tumlinson Electric\u2019s best practices material list, the company protects quality standards, streamlines approvals, and maintains clear communication between Project Management and Field Operations."}]},{"type":"horizontal_rule"},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iSHCMmO"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Policy","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-v2NAi0u"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"1. Submittal Requirements","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zaaCq77"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Before submitting on "},{"type":"text","text":"commodity material","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":", the PM must meet with the foreman\/superintendent to confirm which best practice material will be used on the project."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zaaCq77"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The "},{"type":"text","text":"Project Manager (PM)","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" is responsible for submitting all materials from Tumlinson Electric\u2019s "},{"type":"text","text":"Best Practices Material List","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" on every project, regardless of project-specific specifications."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-D27jBQX"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If a best practices material is not permitted per the project specifications, the PM must submit an "},{"type":"text","text":"RFI (Request for Information)","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" to the design team\/GC requesting approval for use."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-CXFqVy7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"2. Approval & Rejection Tracking","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-9eq7zW2"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"All materials submitted\u2014whether "},{"type":"text","text":"approved, denied, or pending","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":"\u2014 will be logged in the ACC Build RFI module."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-7qjY8k6"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Any materials from the best practices list that are rejected must be documented with the corresponding "},{"type":"text","text":"RFI response or GC\/Engineer directive","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" for record-keeping and noted through mark ups in the project specifications."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6vTJWKm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"3. Communication to Field Teams","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-nDfUoU5"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The PM must communicate the status of all best practices material (approved or not approved) to the "},{"type":"text","text":"Tumlinson Foreman\/Superintendent","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":":"}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-XwMwNkx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"During the "},{"type":"text","text":"project pre-construction meeting","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":", or"}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-YOGnVpi"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Through the "},{"type":"text","text":"weekly project meeting","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" when updates occur."}]}]}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6Qj0Yzu"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"This communication must clearly outline which materials are approved for use, which are pending, and which are rejected, ensuring field crews are working only with approved materials."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-EDyc1YU"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"4. Accountability","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WJSTAyu"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The PM is accountable for ensuring all submittals and RFIs related to best practices materials are tracked and communicated in a timely manner."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-F1HIpSn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Superintendent\/Foreman is responsible for implementing the approved material in the field and reporting any conflicts or discrepancies immediately to the PM."}]}]}]},{"type":"horizontal_rule"},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uYEwGoJ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Summary","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-RoVCzgR"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"By requiring every Project Manager to submit best practices materials on all projects, Tumlinson Electric ensures consistency, quality, and compliance. Rejected materials are managed through the RFI process, and all outcomes are transparently communicated to field leadership, preventing installation errors and rework"}]}]}

steps.name: Pay Stubs
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3,"id":"pm-wObvjxF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Employee HUB","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#2a9292"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-PwHWHej"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tumlinson Electric utilizes a program called Employee HUB. Within the first week of employment, an employee will receive an email to their personal email inviting them to Employee HUB and containing their login information to access. Paysubs and W2s can only be accessed via the Employee HUB. They cannot be emailed nor mailed to the employee. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-dD36O8U"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-xHwrPjX"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If an employee has lost their login information, they need to contact the HR Manager to regain access. ","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":"here","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"deeplink","attrs":{"subject":"sub_0qko7bge3jfdncfrhriiakzntfykfjpp","document":"doc_0rcply16qgxuribebo6loxoqxwcc4w4g","step":"stp_0rcply4odpzpqyull2hc30g03l0ztget"}}]},{"type":"text","text":")","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-h2mcwlN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once identified, the Superintendent\/Foreman must complete an "},{"type":"text","text":"Equipment Penetration Authorization Form","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" and submit it to the TE PM."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-uW31Iu1"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The TE PM will forward the form to the GC for coordination and signature from the equipment supplier."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-40qtbCP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"No penetrations may occur until the Authorization Form is steps.name: Receiving
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bNrGR11"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Initial Condition Documentation:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Take clear photos of the equipment as it arrives, including packaging, labels, and visible part numbers."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-8URjfUF"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Key Components:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Capture images of relevant components, such as control panels, internal parts, connection points, and wiring diagrams provided by the manufacturer. Email all documentation to the GC and the contractor providing the equipment, and save this correspondence in the appropriate file."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qVDzB2d"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Delivery and Location Verification:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" All third-party equipment must be delivered directly to the designated installation location, in collaboration with the delivering party, to avoid unnecessary handling and ensure it is ready for inspection and installation."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sHwA5QI"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Inspection Upon Arrival:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Inspect all third-party equipment upon receipt for visible damage, missing parts, or other discrepancies."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sWaS8VQ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Documentation:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Record the make, model, serial number, and any other identifying information."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-gkeg6Ji"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Receipt Confirmation:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Confirm receipt with the supplier, noting any issues. Email all documentation to the General Contractor (GC) and the contractor providing the equipment. Save this correspondence in the build file designated for such equipment."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ZsbIleK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tilt Requirements Verification:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Prior to moving equipment, verify the manufacturer\u2019s tilt requirements to ensure compliance with warranty specifications. Any handling that does not meet these tilt requirements may void the warranty. This verification is especially critical when moving large or sensitive equipment into mechanical rooms, where tilt issues have arisen on past projects"}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sPEubVs"}}]}

steps.name: Voltage Requirements
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-g5Ms5ow"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Manufacturer Specifications:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Verify voltage and phase requirements by checking the equipment\u2019s label or manual."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-E9abf3U"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Electrical Drawings Verification:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Cross-check the voltage specifications against the electrical drawings for the project."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-mmDgbkV"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Reporting Discrepancies:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Report any discrepancies to the project manager and electrician before installation. Email all documentation related to any discrepancies to the GC and the contractor providing the equipment, and save this correspondence in the appropriate file."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Lk8jqMM"}}]}

steps.name: Voltage Verification Against Electrical Drawings
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-nANizvd"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Pre-Installation Verification:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Before connecting power, verify that the equipment\u2019s voltage requirements match the specifications in the electrical drawings."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JCBWPC7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Coordination with Electrical Contractor:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Work with the electrical contractor to confirm the power supply meets safety requirements."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-rncJ7Fz"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Approval Before Power-Up:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Obtain written confirmation from the electrical contractor and project manager that voltage requirements are accurate before energizing the equipment."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-71VIaTy"}}]}

steps.name: Connection Point with Flex and Conduit
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Ju0kXTw"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Location Identification:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Identify the correct connection point for power based on electrical drawings."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-jwchnPR"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Materials:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Ensure flexible conduit and connectors meet project and safety standards."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SfkO5CU"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Installation:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Securely mount and properly terminate all conduit and connections per local code requirements."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-prQi2hx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\ud83d\udcccImportant","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#cc0606"}}]}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-prQi2hx"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Penetrating Equipment\/Cabinets:","marks":[{"type":"bold"},{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#cc0606"}}]},{"type":"text","text":" If the equipment lacks a removable cover or specific instructions for creating an entry point, notify the GC. Tumlinson\u2019s policy prohibits drilling holes in third-party equipment with electronics, control boards, or other parts that could be damaged by shavings from drilling or the process of pulling wire into such units, unless specific instructions and locations are provided, as this could void warranties and UL listings. Approval through the Tumlinson GS and PM ,from the GC and equipment provider is required before any holes are drilled to maintain enclosure integrity. This includes all equipment and enclosures for such equipment."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-T69Gwz9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"See 3rd Party Equipment Penetrating for more details."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-P3SFJUE"}}]}

steps.name: Pictures of Final Wire Connections

Save all photos in the project file for final approval and compliance."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Yv2Nvgu"}}]}

steps.name: PTO
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":1,"id":"pm-w2z2YpK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PTO Request ","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#2a9292"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-oY5FPqu"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"A PTO request form must be completed and submitted for any employee to utilize PTO. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ciXuoXw"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LvPsmru"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"For the field, a PTO request form must be submitted to both the General Superintendent of the project and the Office Manager. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-J3J9ese"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-phnwhpl"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"So that the Company may schedule work and plan for business requirements, employee requests for PTO for more than three (3) consecutive days should be submitted to his or her General Superintendent at least 30 days in advance unless the PTO is needed for an emergency or illness, which precludes this advance scheduling. PTO can be used at your discretion without prior approval. Examples of this time would be surgery, sick time, funerals, birthdays, vehicle problems, sick family member, etc. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1arpvGT"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-FSVX5xQ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If an employee requests PTO around a company holiday, all requests must be submitted 30 days prior of the requested time off and must be approved. This is required to receive both PTO and the holiday pay. "}]}]}

steps.name: Steps to Reprimand
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":1,"id":"pm-w2z2YpK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Steps to Reprimand","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#2a9292"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-lvOfX8Q"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Before reprimanding any employee, please check with confer with your General Superintendent. "}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-HtpXD37"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"VERBAL REPRIMAND. The first step of the Company\u2019s progressive disciplinary policy is the \u201cverbal reprimand.\u201d This is a verbal warning to an employee that his conduct is unacceptable, and that repeated or continued failure to conform his conduct or performance to the Company standards will result in more severe disciplinary action. Before receiving a verbal reprimand, an employee will be counseled by his supervisor and told what improvements are necessary and expected to correct any performance deficiencies. A record of the notice of the verbal reprimand may be made and retained in the employee\u2019s personnel file."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BAt9Cs9"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"WRITTEN REPRIMAND. The second step is a \u201cwritten reprimand.\u201d This reprimand will describe the unacceptable conduct or performance of the employee and specify needed changes or improvements. A copy of the written reprimand will be retained in the employee\u2019s personnel file."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-k1E1H9g"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"SUSPENSION. Suspension of the employee\u2019s employment may, at the sole discretion of the Company, be used as a third step. The length of the suspension will vary based upon such factors as the severity of the offense, the employee\u2019s performance, and the employee\u2019s disciplinary record. An employee may be suspended for repeated instances of minor misconduct, failure to conform his conduct or performance to the standards of his position, or for a single serious offense. A record of the suspension will be retained in the employee\u2019s personnel file."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zbuhbPv"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"TERMINATION. The final step in the disciplinary procedure is the termination of the employee."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-J3Nmpgd"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-zpwllXM"}}]}

steps.name: Jobsite Theft and Vandalism Policy
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":1,"id":"pm-32yAkuN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Jobsite Theft and Vandalism Policy","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#2a9292"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-wxNhKxp"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"In the event of a break-in, theft, and\/or vandalism on the project site, the following steps must be completed immediately."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-QWmbSRn"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0"}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Pj8Bycr"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Contact local authorities and file\/submit a police report."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-i2mHMHB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Take photos of the vandalized items."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-qEiKd7v"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"An incident report must be submitted to the TE team."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-hfEoSJw"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The incident report must be submitted to the General Contractor\u2019s project team."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-V8wTw9o"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The incident will need to be noted in the project daily log."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-XXbweXl"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-6h4OPkD"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If the General Contractor\/Owner have an issue with requiring a police report, you must notify the TE project team immediately for a response."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-LxoSpBm"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-s0TjcU4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is very important that everything is documented correctly and submitted in a timely manner."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WOKdDQ4"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"\u00a0"}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-0xgrgvB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"It is all our This will help eliminate the chance of them being stolen as well as helping all other projects plan for work to be performed."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-iahIi7Q"}},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bJjyIEj"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Daily log","marks":[{"type":"deeplink","attrs":{"subject":"sub_0qko7bge3jfdncfrhriiakzntfykfjpp","document":"doc_0qkzapsmrjfxiyeh4efjmtdobq3tvorb","step":"stp_0qkzapvereq4cxl6eobgbmv5vmkvemjw"}}]},{"type":"text","text":" and "},{"type":"text","text":"Equipment Los\/Damage\/Theft Incident","marks":[{"type":"deeplink","attrs":{"subject":"sub_0qko7bge3jfdncfrhriiakzntfykfjpp","document":"doc_0qkzaxhylnncz1ttnmggbyslwqmelshx","step":"stp_0qkzaxkoc8taziici6ldaz9ibpncf9pk"}}]},{"type":"text","text":" form are part of this policy. steps.name: Conex Security Action Plan
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":1,"id":"pm-ABPlfXz"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Conex Security Action Plan","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#2a9292"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-dmNDoIS"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If your jobsite requires a Conex, the following actions are required upon ordering your jobsite Conex from the Tumlinson Electric Project Manager or Rental Company:"}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-9Yxm6AH"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Order Conex Security Kit from the Tumlinson Electric Tool Manager. The security kit is required for every Tumlinson Electric Conex on the jobsite."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-cYFPxBB"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"When the Conex arrives on the jobsite, the following must be completed prior to putting anything within the Conex:"}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-r0OknG3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Install the Conex Security Action Plan on the inside wall of the Conex. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-G8oHtix"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Install six (6) Video Surveillance signs:"}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pjEaXCQ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"One (1) at the front door. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-1hvf9h3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"One (1) at the back door. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Vyuc4dN"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Two (2) on each side of the Conex (total of 4 posted)."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sACjx0l"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Install four (4) security cameras. Locate them as you feel needed, but must install four (4). "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pClfrn7"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Install one (1) anti theft vibration alarm on the middle inside of all doors. "}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-UNpiU2J"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"One (1) control panel should stay with the Superintendent\/Foreman. The other control panel should be used as the Superintendent\/Foreman deems necessary. "}]}]}]}]}]}]}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-vDxAjBZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"If at any time any of the above items become damaged, removed or lost, you must reorder the needed replacement parts from the Tumlinson Electric Tool Manager ASAP."}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-kJKox2D"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"When the Conex is ready for the rental company to pick up and has been cleaned out, YOU MUST REMOVE ALL THE CONEX SECURITY KIT ITEMS AND RETURN TO THE TOOL MANAGER."}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Y0Utznq"}}]}

docs.name: Project Specific Drug Testing Procedures for Current and New Field Employees

steps.name: Project Specific Drug Testing Procedures for Current and New Field Employees
steps.content: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3,"id":"pm-b1maCAi"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Project Specific Drug Test Procedures for Current and New Field Employees","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#258d8d"}}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ySGcpPo"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Many projects that Tumlinson is contracted for require mandatory drug testing before an employee is allowed on the job site. In these cases, a \"drug testing letter\" is sent to the general contractor confirming that Tumlinson has drug tested each employee before sending the employee to the jobsite."}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-b1oeCOT"}},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3,"id":"pm-MHn0jpt"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Steps To Take Before Testing:","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#258d8d"}}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-pWQlGR3"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Before an employee\/future employee is drug tested, it must be confirmed that the employee has a completed and signed Drug Test Policy Acknowledgement as part of their onboarding packet on file. The Officer Manager\/Analytical Manager can confirm if the employee completed and is on file. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-sVC9Tcf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The General Superintendent, if needed, will confirm with the Office Manager the type of test to be given that meets the project requirements. This also includes confirmation if an alcohol test is required.  "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-BCtD1BP"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The employee\/future employee will be assigned a time and place for drug and\/or alcohol testing with a General Superintendent or at one of the Tumlinson Electric's contracted drug testing facilities."}]}]}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3,"id":"pm-g9MDCEe"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Procedures for Alcohol\/Drug Testing being Performed by Approved TE Personnel:","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#2a9292"}}]}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-SaoxFlR"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The General Superintendent or authorized representative will ask the employee\/future employee if there are any prescriptions they are taking that could affect the results of the test. If so, the Office Manager should be contacted and the employee\/future employee will be directed to one of the Tumlinson Electric's contracted drug testing facilities for testing. The employee\/future employee will need to take all prescription information with them to the facility. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-0EbjAal"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The General Superintendent or authorized representative will meet with the employee\/future employee to test. They will provide the employee\/future employee with the drug test and instruct them to open the packaging of the sealed drug test. "},{"type":"text","text":"NEVER USE A TEST THAT HAS A BROKEN SEAL.","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-u9YNt0f"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The General Superintendent or authorized representative will instruct the employee\/future employee how much of a sample is needed to properly complete the test. The employee\/future employee is then instructed to go into the restroom and perform the test. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-AkROawM"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"When the employee\/future employee exists the restroom, he\/she must hold the cup so to show the temperature gauge located on the bottom, back side of the testing cup. The General Superintendent or authorized representative is to take a photo of the temperature reading. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-JJdj6KY"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The employee\/future employee then needs to turn the testing cup so that all testing panels are shown and the General Superintendent\/authorized representative can view all the testing lines. The General Superintendent\/authorized representative will take a photo of the testing lines. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ujCdbcf"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"All testing photos are to be EMAILED to both the Office Manager and Vice President, followed by a phone call to assure expedited reading of the results. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-V28BJEZ"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Office Manager and\/or Vice President will approve\/reject the results. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ceDT9Wa"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once the Office Manager and\/or the Vice President approves the results, the Office Manager or Analytical manager will assure the file is updated and any needed letter will be sent out to the TE project team for submission. If the Analytical Manager sends out the letter, the Office Manager needs to be included on the submission so to save to file. "}]}]}]},{"type":"heading","attrs":{"level":3,"id":"pm-1103Fky"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"For Projects Requiring DOT Alcohol Testing:","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#2a9292"}}]}]},{"type":"ordered_list","attrs":{"order":1},"content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-WwfEptX"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The General Superintendent or authorized representative will provide the employee\/future employee with the DOT Alcohol Saliva Screening Test. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Ztg8oTV"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The employee\/future employee will open the test and wet the pad with their saliva for 5 seconds (place in mouth), then remove. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-UYcgmrC"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The General Superintendent or authorized representative will need to wait 4 minutes for the results. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-Z394qu6"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"The General Superintendent or authorized representative will take a photo of the test and EMAIL it to the Office Manager and the Vice President, followed by a phone call to assure expedited reading of the results. "}]}]},{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-bgyhnv0"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Once the Office Manager or Vice President approves the results, the Office Manager or Analytical Manager will assure the file is updated and any needed letter will be sent to the TE project team for submission. If the Analytical Manager sends out the letter, the Office Manager needs to be included on the submission so to save to the file. "}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-aRua62d"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"For projects that require both the DOT alcohol and drug testing, both will be included in one letter. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-l8QrWfN"}}]}

","marks":[{"type":"font_color","attrs":{"color":"#258d8d"}}]}]},{"type":"bullet_list","content":[{"type":"list_item","content":[{"type":"paragraph","attrs":{"id":"pm-ysk2JRK"},"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Procure
